466	\section{Sub-lemmas of the construction}
467	\label{sec:quot_sub_lemmas}
468	
469	\begin{lemma}[Reduction to the universal case]
470	  \label{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}
471	  
472	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Reduction to the case of
473	  % \(\Quot_{\pi^*W/\PP(V)/S}^{\Phi,L}\)"
474	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
475	  % L2294-L2332).
476	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
477	  % "It is enough to prove Theorem REF in the special case
478	  %  that \(X = \PP(V)\) and
479	  %  \(E= \pi^*(W)\) where \(V\) and \(W\) are vector bundles on \(S\), as
480	  %  a consequence of the next lemma.
481	  %
482	  %  \begin{lemma}
483	  %  (i) Let \(\nu\) be any integer.
484	  %  Then tensoring by \(L^{\nu}\) gives an isomorphism of
485	  %  functors from \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) to
486	  %  \(\Quot_{E(\nu)/X/S}^{\Psi,L}\) where the polynomial
487	  %  \(\Psi \in \Q[\lambda]\) is defined by \(\Psi(\lambda) = \Phi(\lambda + \nu)\).
488	  %
489	  %  (ii) Let \(\phi : E \to G\) be a surjective homomorphism of
490	  %  coherent sheaves on \(X\). Then the corresponding natural transformation
491	  %  \(\Quot_{G/X/S}^{\Phi,L} \to \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\)
492	  %  is a closed embedding.
493	  %  \end{lemma}"
494	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
495	  Let \(S\), \(X\), \(L\), \(E\), \(\Phi\) be as in \cref{def:quot_functor}.
496	  \begin{enumerate}
497	    \item (Twist-shift.) For \(\nu \in \mathbb{Z}\), tensoring by
498	      \(L^{\otimes \nu}\) gives an isomorphism of functors
499	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \xrightarrow{\sim}
500	      \Quot^{\Psi,L}_{E(\nu)/X/S}\( with \)\Psi(\lambda) = \Phi(\lambda + \nu)$.
501	    \item (Surjection induces closed embedding.) Given a surjection
502	      \(\phi : E \twoheadrightarrow G\) of coherent sheaves on \(X\), the
503	      induced natural transformation
504	      \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{G/X/S} \to \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) is a closed
505	      embedding of functors.
506	  \end{enumerate}
507	  Consequently, if \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\pi^* W / \mathbb{P}(V)/S}\) is
508	  representable for all \(V\), \(W\) vector bundles on \(S\), then so is
509	  \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) for every \(X \subset \mathbb{P}(V)\) closed and
510	  every coherent \(E\) on \(X\) that is a quotient of \(\pi^*(W)(\nu)|_X\) for
511	  some \(W\) and \(\nu\).
512	\end{lemma}
513	
514	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The statement (i) is obvious. The statement (ii) just
515	% says that given any locally noetherian scheme \(T\) and a family
516	% \(\langle \F,q\rangle \in \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(T)\), there exists
517	% a closed subscheme \(T' \subset T\) with the following universal property:
518	% for any locally noetherian scheme \(U\) and a morphism \(f: U\to T\),
519	% the pulled back homomorphism of \({\mathcal O}_{X_U}\)-modules \(q_U : E_U \to \F_U\)
520	% factors via the pulled back homomorphism \(\phi_U : E_U \to G_U\)
521	% if and only if \(U \to T\) factors via \(T'\hra T\).
522	% This is satisfied by taking \(T'\) to be the vanishing scheme
523	% for the composite homomorphism \(\ker(\phi) \hra  E \stackrel{q}{\to} \F\)
524	% of coherent sheaves on \(X_T\) (see Remark REF),
525	% which makes sense here as both \(\ker(\phi)\) and \(\F\) are coherent
526	% on \(X_T\) and \(\F\) is flat over \(T\)."
527	\begin{proof}
528	  
529	  (i) is direct from the definitions: tensoring with \(L^{\otimes \nu}\) is
530	  an autoequivalence of coherent sheaves on \(X\) (and on every \(X_T\)),
531	  preserving surjectivity, \(T\)-flatness, and properness of support, and
532	  shifting the Hilbert polynomial by \(\nu\) in the variable.
533	
534	  (ii) Given a family \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) on \(T\) in
535	  \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\), the locus where \(q\) factors through
536	  \(\phi : E \twoheadrightarrow G\) is cut out by the vanishing of the
537	  composite $\ker(\phi)|_{X_T} \hookrightarrow E_T \xrightarrow{q}
538	  \mathcal{F}\(. Because \)\mathcal{F}\( is \)T$-flat, the schematic image of
539	  this composite descends to a closed subscheme \(T' \subset T\) such that
540	  \(f : U \to T\) factors through \(T'\) iff \(q_U\) factors through \(\phi_U\).
541	  Hence \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{G/X/S} \hookrightarrow \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\)
542	  is a closed embedding of subfunctors.
543	\end{proof}
544	
545	\begin{lemma}[Uniform Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity bound]
546	  \label{lem:quot_boundedness}
547	  
548	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Use of \(m\)-Regularity"
549	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
550	  % L2346-L2404). Builds on [Nitsure] \S 2 (Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity)
551	  % and \S 3 (semi-continuity and base-change), cited internally as
552	  % Theorem~"Mumford" and Theorem~"Base-change for flat sheaves".
553	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
554	  % "We consider the sheaf \(E = \pi^*(W)\) on \(X=\PP(V)\) where
555	  %  \(V\) is a vector bundle on \(S\),
556	  %  and take \(L = {\mathcal O}_{\PP(V)}(1)\).
557	  %  For any field \(k\) and a \(k\)-valued point
558	  %  \(s\) of \(S\), we have an isomorphism
559	  %  \(\PP(V)_s \simeq \PP^n_k\) where \(n=\rank(V)-1\),
560	  %  and the restricted sheaf \(E_s\) on \(\PP(V)_s\) is isomorphic to
561	  %  \(\oplus^p{\mathcal O}_{\PP(V)_s}\) where \(p=\rank(W)\).
562	  %  It follows from Theorem REF
563	  %  that given any \(\Phi \in \Q[\lambda]\), there exists an integer
564	  %  \(m\) which depends only on \(\rank(V)\), \(\rank(W)\) and \(\Phi\),
565	  %  such that for any field \(k\) and a \(k\)-valued point \(s\) of \(S\),
566	  %  the sheaf \(E_s\) on \(\PP(V)_s\) is \(m\)-regular, and for any
567	  %  coherent quotient \(q: E_s \to \F\) on \(\PP(V)_s\) with
568	  %  Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi\), the sheaf \(\F\) and the kernel sheaf
569	  %  \(\G\subset E_s\) of \(q\) are both \(m\)-regular."
570	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Use of \(m\)-Regularity" (FGA Explained
571	  Ch.~5).}
572	  Let \(X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)\), \(E = \pi^* W\) with \(V\), \(W\) vector bundles on
573	  \(S\) of ranks \(n+1\), \(p\), \(L = \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}(V)}(1)\), and
574	  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). There exists an integer
575	  \(m = m(n, p, \Phi)\), depending only on \(n\), \(p\), and \(\Phi\), such that
576	  for every geometric point \(s\) of \(S\) and every coherent quotient
577	  \(q : E_s \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) with Hilbert polynomial
578	  \(\Phi\), the sheaves \(E_s\), \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(\ker q\) on \(X_s\) are all
579	  \(m\)-regular in the sense of Castelnuovo--Mumford. Consequently, for
580	  every \(T \to S\) and every \(T\)-flat quotient
581	  \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) on \(X_T\) with Hilbert
582	  polynomial \(\Phi\) and every \(r \ge m\), the conclusions \textbf{(*)} and
583	  \textbf{(**)} of \cite[\S 5, "Use of \(m\)-Regularity"]{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
584	  hold: the direct images \((\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)\), \((\pi_T)_* E_T(r)\),
585	  \((\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)\) are locally free of fixed ranks; the
586	  canonical surjections \((\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \twoheadrightarrow (-)(r)\)
587	  hold; and the higher direct images \(R^i (\pi_T)_* (-)(r)\) vanish for
588	  \(i \ge 1\).
589	\end{lemma}
590	
591	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see [Nitsure], \S 5, "Use of \(m\)-Regularity"
592	% (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L2346-L2404).
593	% The proof invokes the m-regularity theorem of [Nitsure] \S 2
594	% (Theorem "Mumford") to extract the uniform bound m, and the
595	% base-change-for-flat-sheaves theorem of [Nitsure] \S 3 (cited as
596	% "Base-change for flat sheaves") to propagate the conclusions across \(T\).
597	\begin{proof}
598	  
599	  Each geometric fiber \(E_s\) is a trivial bundle on \(\mathbb{P}^n_{\kappa(s)}\)
600	  of rank \(p\), so \(E_s\) is \(0\)-regular. The Castelnuovo--Mumford
601	  \(m\)-regularity theorem of [Nitsure] \S 2 produces, for any fixed Hilbert
602	  polynomial \(\Phi\), a uniform integer \(m = m(n, p, \Phi)\) above which
603	  every coherent quotient of \(E_s\) with Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi\) -- and
604	  its kernel -- is \(m\)-regular. The Castelnuovo Lemma of [Nitsure] \S 2
605	  converts \(m\)-regularity into vanishing of \(H^i((-) (r))\) for \(i \ge 1\)
606	  and \(r \ge m\), and into global generation of \(H^0((-)(r))\). To
607	  propagate the fiber-wise statement to families over \(T\), apply the
608	  flat-base-change theorem (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}, Stacks
609	  tag 02KH) along \(T \to S\): cohomology of flat families commutes with
610	  base change, so the direct images \((\pi_T)_*(-)(r)\) are locally free of
611	  the rank dictated by the fiber Hilbert polynomials, and the canonical
612	  maps \((\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \to (-)(r)\) inherit fiberwise
613	  surjectivity.
614	\end{proof}
615	
616	\begin{lemma}[Grassmannian embedding \(\alpha\) is injective]
617	  \label{lem:quot_alpha_injective}
618	  
619	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"
620	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
621	  % L2409-L2451).
622	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
623	  % "We now fix a positive integer \(r\) such that \(r\ge m\).
624	  %  Note that the rank of \({\pi_T}_*\F(r)\) is \(\Phi(r)\)
625	  %  and \(\pi_*E(r) = W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V\).
626	  %  Therefore the surjective homomorphism
627	  %  \({\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)\)
628	  %  defines an element of the set
629	  %  \(\Grass( W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V , \Phi(r))(T)\).
630	  %  We thus get a morphism of functors
631	  %  \(\)\alpha : \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \to
632	  %  \Grass( W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V, \Phi(r))\(\)
633	  %  It associates to \(q: E_T \to \F\) the quotient
634	  %  \({\pi_T}_*(q(r)) : {\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)\).
635	  %
636	  %  The above morphism \(\alpha\) is injective because the quotient
637	  %  \(q: E_T \to \F\) can be recovered from
638	  %  \({\pi_T}_*(q(r)) : {\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)\)
639	  %  as follows."
640	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"
641	  (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
642	  In the universal setup \(X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)\), \(E = \pi^* W\), fix
643	  \(r \ge m\) from \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}. The natural transformation
644	  \[
645	    \alpha :
646	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \longrightarrow
647	    \mathrm{Grass}\!\left(
648	      W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V,\; \Phi(r)
649	    \right),
650	    \qquad
651	    \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle \longmapsto
652	    \langle (\pi_T)_*\mathcal{F}(r),\, (\pi_T)_*(q(r))\rangle
653	  \]
654	  is well-defined (the target is locally free of rank \(\Phi(r)\), and the
655	  canonical isomorphism $\pi_* E(r) \cong W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S}
656	  \mathrm{Sym}^r V\( gives the source) and \emph{injective} on \)T$-points:
657	  the data of the locally free quotient at level \(r\) determines
658	  \(q : E_T \to \mathcal{F}\).
659	\end{lemma}
660	
661	\begin{proof}
662	  
663	  Suppose \(\alpha(T)\) sends both \(\langle \mathcal{F}_1, q_1\rangle\) and
664	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}_2, q_2\rangle\) to the same \(T\)-point of the
665	  Grassmannian. Then the pulled-back kernel
666	  $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}_i(r) \to (\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* E_T(r)
667	  = \pi_{T_0}^* (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V)_T$
668	  is the same. The diagram \textbf{(**)} of
669	  \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} gives a right-exact sequence
670	  \[
671	    (\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}_i(r) \xrightarrow{h}
672	    E_T(r) \xrightarrow{q_i(r)} \mathcal{F}_i(r) \to 0,
673	  \]
674	  so \(q_i(r)\) is recovered as the cokernel of \(h\); twisting by \(-r\)
675	  recovers \(q_i\), giving \(q_1 = q_2\) and hence
676	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}_1, q_1\rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}_2, q_2\rangle\).
677	\end{proof}
678	
679	\begin{lemma}[Valuative criterion of properness for \(\Quot\)]
680	  \label{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}
681	  
682	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
683	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
684	  % L2510-L2542). Original reference: EGA IV(2) 2.8.1, cited there.
685	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
686	  % "The functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) satisfies the following valuative
687	  %  criterion for properness over \(S\): given any discrete valuation ring
688	  %  \(R\) over \(S\) with quotient field \(K\), the restriction map
689	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R) \to \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)\(\)
690	  %  is bijective. This can be seen as follows.
691	  %  Given any coherent quotient
692	  %  \(q: E_K \to \F\) on \(X_R\) which defines an element \(\langle \F, q\rangle\)
693	  %  of \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)\).
694	  %  Let \(\ov{\F}\) be the image of the composite homomorphism
695	  %  \(E_R \to j_*(E_K) \to j_*\F\) where \(j : X_K \hra X_R\) is the open
696	  %  inclusion. Let \(\ov{q} : E_R\to \ov{\F}\) be the induced surjection.
697	  %  Then we leave it to the reader to verify that
698	  %  \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) is an element of \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R)\)
699	  %  which maps to \(\langle \F, q\rangle\), and is the unique such element.
700	  %  (Use the basic fact that being flat over a dvr
701	  %  is the same as being torsion-free.)"
702	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
703	  (FGA Explained Ch.~5); EGA IV(2) 2.8.1.}
704	  The functor \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) satisfies the valuative criterion
705	  for properness over \(S\): for every discrete valuation ring \(R\) with
706	  fraction field \(K\) and every morphism \(\Spec R \to S\), the restriction
707	  map
708	  \[
709	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec R)
710	    \longrightarrow
711	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec K)
712	  \]
713	  is bijective.
714	\end{lemma}
715	
716	\begin{proof}
717	  
718	  Given a \(\Spec K\)-point \(\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle\) on \(X_K\),
719	  let \(j : X_K \hookrightarrow X_R\) be the open inclusion. Define
720	  \(\overline{\mathcal{F}}\) as the image of the composite
721	  \(E_R \to j_* E_K \to j_* \mathcal{F}_K\), with induced surjection
722	  \(\overline{q} : E_R \to \overline{\mathcal{F}}\). The sheaf
723	  \(\overline{\mathcal{F}}\) is torsion-free over \(R\) (being a subsheaf of
724	  \(j_* \mathcal{F}_K\)), hence \(R\)-flat (flatness over a DVR
725	  \(=\) torsion-freeness), and restricts on the generic fiber to
726	  \(\mathcal{F}_K\). The pair \((\overline{\mathcal{F}}, \overline{q})\) is
727	  the unique extension of \(\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle\) to a
728	  \(\Spec R\)-point (uniqueness: any other extension agrees on the dense
729	  open \(X_K \subset X_R\) and is torsion-free, hence equals the image
730	  construction). The Hilbert polynomial is constant on connected
731	  \(\Spec R\) by flatness.
732	\end{proof}

734	\section{Cohomology and base change}
735	\label{sec:cohomology_base_change}
736	
737	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
738	boundedness step (\cref{lem:quot_boundedness}) and the Grassmannian
739	embedding (\cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective}). We record the relevant
740	statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it directly.
741	
742	\begin{theorem}\leanok
743	[Flat base change of cohomology]
744	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
745	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology}
746	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology)

754	  %  }
755	  %  \(\)
756	  %  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
757	  %  with pullback \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\).
758	  %  Assume that \(g\) is flat and that \(f\) is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
759	  %  For any \(i \geq 0\)
760	  %  \begin{enumerate}
761	  %  \item the base change map of
762	  %  Cohomology, Lemma REF
763	  %  is an isomorphism
764	  %  \(\)
765	  %  g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
766	  %  \(\)
767	  %  \item if \(S = \Spec(A)\) and \(S' = \Spec(B)\), then
768	  %  \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\).
769	  %  \end{enumerate}"
770	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology).}
771	  Let
772	  \[
773	    \begin{array}{ccc}
774	      X' & \xrightarrow{g'} & X \\
775	      \downarrow f' &  & \downarrow f \\
776	      S' & \xrightarrow{g} & S
777	    \end{array}
778	  \]
779	  be a cartesian diagram of schemes with \(g\) flat and \(f\) quasi-compact and
780	  quasi-separated. Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent
781	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module with pull-back
782	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^* \mathcal{F}\). For every \(i \ge 0\):
783	  \begin{enumerate}
784	    \item the base-change map
785	      \(g^* R^i f_* \mathcal{F} \to R^i f'_* \mathcal{F}'\) is an isomorphism;
786	    \item if \(S = \Spec A\) and \(S' = \Spec B\), then $H^i(X, \mathcal{F})
787	      \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
788	  \end{enumerate}
789	\end{theorem}
790	
791	\subsection{Project-side base-change substrate}
792	\label{subsec:quot_project_basechange}
793	
794	The Lean encoding of \cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology} (and the
795	Quot construction's iso between pushforward and tensor-pullback for the
796	universal section pattern) relies on four project-side declarations
797	introduced in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. These
798	are typed-sorry today: each carries a substantive Lean type that
799	encodes the intended mathematical statement, with the body deferred to
800	later iters (see \cref{sec:quot_lean_encoding} for the

801	phase-by-phase plan).
802	
803	\begin{definition}\leanok
804	[Canonical base-change map on sheaves of modules]
805	  \label{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map}
806	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.canonicalBaseChangeMap}
807	  \uses{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
808	  Given a cartesian square of schemes
809	  \(\square = (g' : X' \to X,\ f' : X' \to S',\ f : X \to S,\ g : S' \to S)\)
810	  with \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\) and \(g\) flat, the
811	  \emph{canonical base-change map} on sheaves of modules is the natural
812	  transformation \(g'^* f^* \mathcal{F} \longrightarrow f'^* g^* \mathcal{F}\)
813	  arising from the universal property of pullback. Encoded as a morphism
814	  of \(\mathrm{ModuleCat}\)-valued sheaf functors on the pulled-back open
815	  cover; this is the morphism whose iso-status discharges
816	  \cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}(1) in the present
817	  finite-locally-free / quasi-coherent-of-finite-type setting.
818	\end{definition}
819	
820	\begin{theorem}\leanok
821	[Stalkwise base-change isomorphism on affines]
822	  \label{thm:quot_canonical_basechange_app_app_isIso}
823	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso}
824	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map, thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
825	  For every affine open \(V \subseteq S'\) and every affine open
826	  \(U \subseteq X\) lying over \(V\), the local component
827	  \((\text{canonicalBaseChangeMap}\ \square).\text{app}\ \mathcal{F}.\text{app}\ U\)
828	  of \cref{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map} is an isomorphism. Proof
829	  reduces (Stacks 02KH(2)) to the affine-base case: \(H^0\) commutes with
830	  flat extension of scalars on a quasi-compact quasi-separated affine
831	  morphism.
832	\end{theorem}
833	
834	\begin{theorem}\leanok
835	[Canonical base-change map is a sheaf isomorphism]
836	  \label{thm:quot_canonical_basechange_isIso}
837	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso}
838	  \uses{thm:quot_canonical_basechange_app_app_isIso}
839	  Globalising the stalk-wise statement
840	  \cref{thm:quot_canonical_basechange_app_app_isIso}: the natural
841	  transformation \(g'^* f^* \mathcal{F} \to f'^* g^* \mathcal{F}\) of
842	  \cref{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map} is an isomorphism of
843	  quasi-coherent sheaves of modules. This is the sheaf-level form of
844	  \cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}(1).
845	\end{theorem}
846	
847	\begin{definition}\leanok
848	[Tensor-presentation of the pullback-of-sections functor]
849	  \label{def:quot_pullback_app_isoTensor}
850	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback_app_isoTensor}
851	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map}
852	  Given a morphism \(g : X \to Y\) of schemes, affine opens
853	  \(U \subseteq Y\), \(V \subseteq X\) with \(g(V) \subseteq U\), and a
854	  quasi-coherent sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{N}\) on \(Y\), the
855	  \emph{tensor-presentation iso} packages the canonical iso
856	  \(\Gamma(V,\, g^* \mathcal{N}) \;\cong\;
857	   \Gamma(V,\, \mathcal{O}_X) \otimes_{\Gamma(U,\, \mathcal{O}_Y)}
858	     \Gamma(U,\, \mathcal{N})\)
859	  as a `LinearEquiv`. This is the project-side rfl-bridge used by the
860	  consumer \(\text{Quot}\)-functor iso assembly: it discharges the
861	  pullback-app step of the Tilde-isoTop route (Stacks tag 01HQ / 01I8)
862	  to a base-change-of-scalars identity. The body factors as
863	  (Step 1, axiom-clean): the underlying linear map
864	  \texttt{pullback\_app\_isoTensor\_unitAtV};
865	  (Step 2, project-side typed sorry): the
866	  \texttt{IsBaseChange}-packaging step

871	\begin{definition}
872	
873	\leanok
874	[\(\Sigma\)-pair packaging: section-level LinearEquiv + intertwining]
875	  \label{def:pullback_app_isoTensor_sigma}
876	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv}
877	  \uses{def:quot_pullback_app_isoTensor, def:quot_canonical_basechange_map,
878	        lem:pullback_tildeIso, lem:tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen,
879	        lem:pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict}
880	  Iter-188 Lane F-introduced \(\Sigma\)-pair packaging declaration that
881	  bundles BOTH the section-level LinearEquiv
882	  \(\Gamma(V,(g^*\mathcal N)) \;\simeq_{\Gamma(Y,U)}\; \Gamma(Y,U) \otimes
883	    \Gamma(N,V)\) AND its intertwining property with the canonical
884	  base-change map into a single \(\Sigma\)-struct returned via
885	  \texttt{Nonempty} for \texttt{IsBaseChange.of\_equiv} consumption.
886	  Concretely:
887	  \[
888	    \exists\, (f : \Gamma(V,(\text{pullback}\, g).\mathrm{obj}\,
889	                     \mathcal N) \;\simeq_{\Gamma(Y,U)}\;
890	                     \Gamma(X,V) \otimes_{\Gamma(Y,U)} \Gamma(N,V)),\;
891	      \forall x,\; f(1\otimes x)\;=\;\text{baseMap}\,g\,\mathcal N\,e\,x.
892	  \]

894	  side bridge into Mathlib's \texttt{IsBaseChange.of\_equiv} consumer.

899	  naturality).

1074	\section{Lean encoding}
1075	\label{sec:quot_lean_encoding}
1076	
1077	The Lean target file is the new
1078	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. The construction
1079	proceeds in three phases.
1080	
1081	\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
1082	On a finite-type morphism \(\pi : X \to S\) with \(S\) noetherian and a
1083	coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) with proper support over \(S\), the per-fiber
1084	Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function $s \mapsto
1085	\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. Mathlib provides
1086	\texttt{CategoryTheory.PolynomialModule} and Euler-characteristic
1087	infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian schemes; the polynomial
1088	property of \(m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})\) requires
1089	Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
1090	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.
1091	
1092	\paragraph{Phase 2: Grassmannian scheme (\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme},
1093	\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).} The Grassmannian
1094	\(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) is encoded as the relative gluing of \(\binom{r}{d}\)
1095	affine charts \(U^I\) (each isomorphic to a relative affine space
1096	\(\mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_S\)) via the Pl\"ucker cocycle \(\theta_{I,J}\). The
1097	gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData}. The
1098	universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) is glued by the transition cocycle
1099	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the determinant line bundle
1100	\(\det \mathcal{U}\) gives the Pl\"ucker closed embedding into
1101	\(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\). The representability statement uses
1102	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} (as in
1103	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}) on the universal pair
1104	\((\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d), \mathcal{U})\).
1105	
1106	\paragraph{Phase 3: Quot scheme (\cref{thm:quot_representable}).}
1107	The Quot functor is constructed as a locally closed subfunctor of the
1108	Grassmannian
1109	\(\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))\), via:
1110	\begin{enumerate}
1111	  \item the uniform \(m\)-regularity bound from
1112	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} (input: Castelnuovo--Mumford theorem,
1113	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.CastelnuovoMumford} -- project-side sub-build);
1114	  \item the Grassmannian embedding \(\alpha\) from
1115	    \cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective};
1116	  \item the flattening stratification (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
1117	    of \cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}, A.2.a) applied to the
1118	    universal quotient on the Grassmannian, producing the locally closed
1119	    stratum \(T_0^\Phi\);
1120	  \item the valuative-criterion upgrade
1121	    (\cref{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}) to closed embedding, hence
1122	    projective \(S\)-scheme.
1123	\end{enumerate}
1124	The reduction \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W} from the universal
1125	case to the general case is encoded as a closed embedding of representable
1126	subfunctors via the kernel-vanishing scheme.
1127	
1128	\paragraph{Cohomology and base change.}
1129	\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology} is the Mathlib-equivalent of
1130	Stacks tag 02KH. Mathlib carries a partial form
1131	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flat\_base\_change}) for affine bases; the
1132	full \(R^i f_*\) statement is a project-side bridge if not present at the
1133	pinned commit.
1134	
1135	\paragraph{Dependencies summary.}
1136	This file directly consumes
1137	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} (A.1.a, on disk) for relative-spectrum
1138	gluing infrastructure and
1139	\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} from
1140	\cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification} (A.2.a, written this iter)
1141	for the flattening stratum. Downstream, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}
1142	(A.2.c) consumes \cref{thm:quot_representable} via the special case
1143	\(E = \mathcal{O}_C\) on a smooth proper curve \(C/k\), giving the Hilbert
1144	scheme \(\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot^{\Phi,\mathcal{O}_C(1)}_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}\)
1145	and its open subscheme \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k}\) of relative effective divisors.
1146	
1147	\section{Out of scope}
1148	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
1149	
1150	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-scheme existence statement and
1151	its proof skeleton via the Grassmannian + flattening-stratification
1152	route. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
1153	\begin{itemize}
1154	  \item \textbf{Flattening stratification} (A.2.a) -- consumed-by, not
1155	    proved-here. Its blueprint home is the new chapter
1156	    \texttt{Picard\_FlatteningStratification.tex}.
1157	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
1158	    \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) is the special case
1159	    \(\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\) of the Quot scheme and is
1160	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
1161	  \item \textbf{Castelnuovo--Mumford \(m\)-regularity theorem} -- input to
1162	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}, not proved here. The proof lives in
1163	    Nitsure \S 2; for the Lean encoding it is a project-side sub-build.
1164	  \item \textbf{Semi-continuity and base change} (Nitsure \S 3) -- the
1165	    flat-base-change theorem of cohomology (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology})
1166	    is stated here as a lemma, but the upstream Mathlib bridge / project-side
1167	    sub-build is not constructed in this chapter.
1168	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
1169	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
1170	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
1171	    project-side sub-build for the Lean encoding.
1172	  \item \textbf{Identification with \(\mathbb{P}^n\) and relative
1173	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --

65	## Decisions identified
66	
67	### Decision 1: `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` vs `of_lift_unique` vs `ofEquiv`
68	
69	- **Mathlib idiom**: For "I have an explicit equiv `S ⊗[R] M ≃ₗ[S] N`
70	  and a 1-tensor intertwining property", the canonical constructor is
71	  `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` (`IsTensorProduct.lean:394`). The 1-tensor
72	  intertwining `∀ x, e (1 ⊗ₜ x) = f x` is exactly the data needed.
73	
74	  `IsBaseChange.of_lift_unique` is for the *universal-property* path:
75	  you must produce, for every target `Q`, a unique extension of every
76	  `R`-linear `M → Q` to an `S`-linear `N → Q`. That is structurally
77	  harder to discharge when the equiv is constructed concretely on the
78	  Spec side.
79	
80	  `IsBaseChange.ofEquiv` (capital E) only handles `e : M ≃ₗ[R] N`
81	  (same scalar ring) — gives `IsBaseChange R e.toLinearMap`. Inapplicable
82	  to Lane F's `R = Γ(X,V) → S = Γ(Y,U)` setup.
83	
84	- **Project's current path** (`QuotScheme.lean:672-695`): produces a
85	  `Nonempty {f : Γ(X,V) ⊗ Γ(N,V) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y,U)] Γ((pullback g).obj N, U) //
86	   ∀ x, f (1 ⊗ₜ x) = pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap g N e x}` via the
87	  named helper, then dispatches `IsBaseChange.of_equiv equiv hApp`.
88	
89	- **Gap**: identical. The project's path is exactly the Mathlib idiom.
90	
91	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** — `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` is the correct API
92	  path. The other constructors are structurally heavier or inapplicable.
93	
94	### Decision 2: `Nonempty Σ-pair` packaging idiom
95	
96	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib has no `LinearEquiv.intertwines` predicate.
97	  The closest idioms are:
98	  (a) Return data + Prop as TWO declarations (a `noncomputable def`
99	      producing the equiv, a `theorem` recording the intertwining).
100	  (b) Return `∃ f : LinearEquiv ..., ∀ x, ...` (a Prop with a Type-valued
101	      witness — perfectly legal since `LinearEquiv` is a `Type`, not
102	      `Prop`).
103	  (c) Return `Nonempty {f : LinearEquiv // ∀ x, ...}` — the project's
104	      current choice. Definitionally a heavier wrapping of (b).
105	
106	- **Project's current path**: option (c). Consumer in
107	  `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_isBaseChange` (L691) unpacks via
108	  `obtain ⟨equiv, hApp⟩ := ...` then feeds into `IsBaseChange.of_equiv`.
109	
110	- **Gap**: divergent-equivalent. (c) and (b) are interchangeable; (c)
111	  is mildly noisier (an extra `Subtype.mk` layer when constructing).
112	  Mathlib leans toward (b) for pure-existence statements (`∃ e, ...`).
113	  Once the helper body is closed, the natural endpoint is *not* (b)
114	  or (c) but (a): a `noncomputable def` for the equiv + a `simp` lemma
115	  for `(1 ⊗ₜ x) = baseMap x`. But while the body is a typed sorry,
116	  packaging as `Nonempty` (which only asserts inhabitedness, not
117	  computability) is the right hygiene.
118	
119	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with current `Nonempty {f // ...}` packaging.
120	  Optionally consider switching to bare `∃ f, ...` for syntactic
121	  parsimony (saves ~1 LOC in consumer obtain pattern). NOT a blocker.
122

The "extract a presentation of `(N\|_V).overSpec` from `[N.IsQuasicoherent]`" sub-step is a **Mathlib gap** (Stacks 01I8: QC ⟺ tilde on every affine open). `IsQuasicoherent` only supplies a cover + presentations on COVER elements (`Quasicoherent.lean:208`), not on a chosen affine open `V`. | NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL |

Direct LSP searches (`pullback.*tilde`, `tilde.*pullback`, `baseChange.*tilde`) return zero matches. 142	### Decision 4: Pin Steps 1 and 3 as separate typed sorries (refactor)
143	
144	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib's pattern for substantive missing
145	  infrastructure is one named declaration per Stacks tag, so future
146	  porting-to-Mathlib lands one PR per tag.
147	
148	- **Project's current path**: All three Mathlib gaps (Steps 1, 2, 3)
149	  are conceptually independent, but only Step 2 is split out. Steps 1
150	  and 3 are buried inside `_sectionLinearEquiv`'s body sorry, making
151	  the `K=4 iters / 9→11 sorry creep / 6 helpers / PARTIAL×4` STUCK
152	  pattern (per iter-188 progress-critic) hard to break: the prover
153	  cannot make progress on `_sectionLinearEquiv` because closing it
154	  requires *three* substantive Stacks lemmas, only ONE of which has a
155	  name to put in scope.
156	
157	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. Cost: provers iterate on the bundled
158	  helper without a way to localize which Stacks gap is the residual
159	  obstruction; the typed-sorry budget keeps growing without progress.
160	
161	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — split out Step 1 and Step 3 as
162	  separately-named typed-sorry pins, parallel to `pullback_tildeIso`
163	  for Step 2. After this refactor, the body of `_sectionLinearEquiv`
164	  becomes pure compositional glue with no internal Mathlib gaps, and
165	  prover work can focus on the three named pins independently.

218	**Overall verdict**: **(A) STRUCTURAL OK** with a critical refactor
219	recommendation. The `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` choice is structurally

221	been STUCK across 4 iters is *not* because the API path is wrong, but

310	end Functorial
311	
312	noncomputable section Restriction
313	
314	variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]
315	
316	/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
317	This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
318	but has better defeqs. -/
319	def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
320	  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
321	  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
322	    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩
323	
324	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
325	abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
326	  (restrictFunctor f).obj M
327	
328	@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
329	    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl
330	
331	@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
332	    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl
333	
334	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
335	def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
337	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
338	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
339	      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
340	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
341	      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
342	
343	/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
344	def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
345	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
346	  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
347	  · ext U x
348	    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
350	      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
351	    exact congr($this x)
352	
353	instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
354	  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)
355	
356	instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
357	instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
358	instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful
359	
360	@[simp]
361	lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
362	    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
363	      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl
364	
365	@[simp]
366	lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
367	    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
368	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl
369	
370	/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
371	def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
372	  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
373	
374	/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
375	def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
376	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
377	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
378	      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
379	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
380	
381	@[simp]
382	lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
383	    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
384	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl
385	
386	@[simp]
387	lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
388	    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
389	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl
390	
391	/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
392	def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
393	  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
394	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
395	    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
396	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
397	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
398	    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm
399

626	/-- **Step 3 pin (transport)**: section-level transport for pullback along
627	the affine-open's `fromSpec` map.
628	
629	iter-189 Lane F unbundling (per `analogies/lane-f-isbasechange.md`
630	Decision 4): pinned as a separately-named typed sorry parallel to
631	`pullback_tildeIso` (Step 2) and `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen`
632	(Step 1).
633	
634	This pin captures the Step 3 transport content of the Tilde-isoTop route:
635	the top section of a sheaf pulled back along
636	`IsAffineOpen.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y` is canonically `Γ(Y, U)`-linearly
637	identified with the section over `U` itself. Substantive content combines
638	`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.isoTop` (Mathlib HAS) with the `hU.isoSpec`
639	transport (Mathlib gap at `b80f227`).
640	
641	iter-190+ body work (~30–50 LOC): `hU.toSpecΓ_fromSpec` + naturality of
642	`tilde.isoTop` + module-pullback-along-open-immersion-is-restriction. -/
643	private theorem pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
644	    {Y : Scheme.{u}} (N : Y.Modules) {U : Y.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :
645	    -- `Γ(Y, U)`-linear identification between the top section of the pullback
646	    -- (along `hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y`) and `Γ(N, U)` itself. The
647	    -- module-action ring on the LHS is set up via the canonical algebra
648	    -- `Γ(Y, U) → Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤)`, which is the structure-sheaf
649	    -- equivalence on the affine scheme.
650	    letI : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
651	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
652	    letI : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
653	      Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom

private theorem pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
    {Y : Scheme.{u}} (N : Y.Modules) {U : Y.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :
    -- `Γ(Y, U)`-linear identification between the top section of the pullback
    -- (along `hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y`) and `Γ(N, U)` itself. The
    -- module-action ring on the LHS is set up via the canonical algebra
    -- `Γ(Y, U) → Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤)`, which is the structure-sheaf
    -- equivalence on the affine scheme.
    letI : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
      Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
    Nonempty (Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]
      Γ(N, U)) := by

    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra

  have hImg : (hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) : Y.Opens) = U := by

  -- iter-190+ body: Spec-transport via `hU.toSpecΓ_fromSpec` + naturality

letI algInst : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
  letI modInst : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
    Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
  -- Step 1: Identify pullback along `hU.fromSpec` with the restriction functor.
  -- Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` gives this for any open immersion;
  -- `hU.fromSpec` is an open immersion by `IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`.
  have isoSheaf : (Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N ≅ N.restrict hU.fromSpec :=
    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hU.fromSpec).app N).symm
  -- Step 2: The image of ⊤ under hU.fromSpec equals U (Stacks 01HH-style bridge).
  have hImg : (hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) : Y.Opens) = U := by
    rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]; exact hU.opensRange_fromSpec
  -- Step 3: section-level map from the iso, then the rfl identification
  -- `Γ(N.restrict hU.fromSpec, ⊤) = Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)` (per
  -- `Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`), then a presheaf restriction along the
  -- propositional equality `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` to land in `Γ(N, U)`.
  -- The forward map at the section level (additive).
  let fhom : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) →+
      Γ(N, U) :=
    AddMonoidHom.comp
      (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by rw [hImg] : U = hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)).op).hom.toAddMonoidHom
      (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom.toAddMonoidHom
  -- The backward map.
  let ghom : Γ(N, U) →+
      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
    AddMonoidHom.comp
      (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom.toAddMonoidHom
      (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by rw [hImg] : hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U)).op).hom.toAddMonoidHom
  -- The package: we'll build the LinearEquiv from these, using:
  -- * additive bijection (fhom, ghom form an inverse pair),
  -- * Γ(Y, U)-linearity (via the natural action on Γ(N, U), and the
  --   compHom-action on the LHS).
  exact ⟨sorry⟩

-- The forward map at the section level (additive).
  let fhom : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) →+
      Γ(N, U) :=

      (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by rw [hImg] : U = hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)).op).hom
      (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom
  -- The backward map.
  let ghom : Γ(N, U) →+
      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
    AddMonoidHom.comp
      (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom
      (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by rw [hImg] : hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U)).op).hom
  -- The package: we'll build the LinearEquiv from these, using:

-- Equality lemma: `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` as `Y.Opens`.
  have hImg' : (hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) : Y.Opens) = U := hImg
  -- The presheaf restriction map from `Γ(N, U)` to
  -- `Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)` (and inverse).
  let presR : Γ(N, U) →+ Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens)) :=
    (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by rw [hImg]) : (⟨hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤, _⟩ : Y.Opensᵒᵖ).unop ⟶ U).op).hom
  -- Define the additive equivalence.
  let addEq : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) ≃+ Γ(N, U) := by
    refine AddEquiv.mk' ⟨⟨fun x =>
        (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom x),
      fun y =>
        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom ((N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op).hom y),
      ?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
    · intro x
      show (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom
        ((N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op).hom
          ((N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom
            ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom x))) = x
      rw [← AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans,
        eqToHom_refl, op_id, Functor.map_id, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
        ← AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, isoSheaf.hom_inv_id,
        Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
    · intro y
      show (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom
        ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom
          ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom
            ((N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op).hom y))) = y
      rw [← AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, isoSheaf.inv_hom_id,
        Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
        ← AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans,
        eqToHom_refl, op_id, Functor.map_id, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
    · intro x y
      show (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom
        ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom (x + y)) =
        (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom
          ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom x) +
        (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom
          ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom y)
      simp [map_add]
  -- Upgrade to a `Γ(Y, U)`-LinearEquiv via the smul compatibility.
  refine ⟨addEq.toLinearEquiv ?_⟩
  -- Smul-compatibility:
  intro r x
  show (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom
    ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom (r • x)) =
    r • (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom
      ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom x)
  -- The LHS `r • x` is `Module.compHom`-action: `r • x = (ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r • x`
  -- with the natural Γ(Spec Γ(Y, U), ⊤)-action on the pullback module sheaf
  -- at ⊤.
  -- Hom.app is Γ(Spec Γ(Y, U), ⊤)-linear via `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul`.
  -- The presheaf map is Y.presheaf-semilinear; combined with the
  -- `fromSpec_app_self`-style compatibility this gives the claim.
  sorry

650	private theorem pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict

653	    -- (along `hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y`) and `Γ(N, U)` itself. The
654	    -- module-action ring on the LHS is set up via the canonical algebra
655	    -- `Γ(Y, U) → Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤)`, which is the structure-sheaf
656	    -- equivalence on the affine scheme.
657	    letI : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
658	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
659	    letI : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
660	      Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
661	    Nonempty (Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]
662	      Γ(N, U)) := by
663	  letI algInst : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
664	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
665	  letI modInst : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
666	    Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
667	  -- Step 1: Identify pullback along `hU.fromSpec` with the restriction functor.
668	  -- Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` gives this for any open immersion;
669	  -- `hU.fromSpec` is an open immersion by `IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`.
670	  have isoSheaf : (Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N ≅ N.restrict hU.fromSpec :=
671	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hU.fromSpec).app N).symm
672	  -- Step 2: The image of ⊤ under hU.fromSpec equals U (Stacks 01HH-style bridge).
673	  have hImg : (hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) : Y.Opens) = U := by
674	    rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]; exact hU.opensRange_fromSpec
675	  -- Step 3: section-level map from the iso, then the rfl identification
676	  -- `Γ(N.restrict hU.fromSpec, ⊤) = Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)` (per
677	  -- `Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`), then a presheaf restriction along the
678	  -- propositional equality `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` to land in `Γ(N, U)`.
679	  -- Define the additive equivalence.

681	    (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom x)
682	  let invFun : Γ(N, U) → Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) := fun y =>
683	    (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom ((N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op).hom y)
684	  have left_inv : Function.LeftInverse invFun toFun := by

687	      eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
688	      AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
689	      ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, isoSheaf.hom_inv_id, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app]
690	  have right_inv : Function.RightInverse invFun toFun := by
691	    intro y

693	      isoSheaf.inv_hom_id, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app,
694	      AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
695	      ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id,
696	      CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]

704	      (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) y from

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

## Lane F Step 3 (`pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict`, line 650)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Use `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback` to identify the
  pullback functor with the restrict functor along the open immersion
  `hU.fromSpec`. Combine with the rfl identification
  `Γ(N.restrict f, ⊤) = Γ(N, f ''ᵁ ⊤)` (`restrict_obj`) and the propositional
  `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` (from `Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange` +
  `IsAffineOpen.opensRange_fromSpec`) to build the section-level `≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]`.

- **Result:** PARTIAL — substantial progress; one residual `sorry` on the
  ring-level compatibility. The full chain that was constructed:
  1. **Sheaf-level iso:** `isoSheaf : (pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N ≅ N.restrict hU.fromSpec` via the symmetric of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`.
  2. **Image equality:** `hImg : hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` via the chain
     `image_top_eq_opensRange + opensRange_fromSpec`.
  3. **Underlying `AddEquiv`:** Built explicitly via the four steps
     (fwd, bwd, left/right inverses, map_add). The left/right inverses
     close axiom-clean via `simp` chains involving
     `← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply`, `← Functor.map_comp`, `← op_comp`,
     `eqToHom_trans`, `eqToHom_refl`, `op_id`, `CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id`,
     `AddCommGrpCat.hom_id`, `AddMonoidHom.id_apply`,
     `← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app`, `isoSheaf.hom_inv_id` / `inv_hom_id`,
     and `Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app`.
  4. **Upgrade to `LinearEquiv`** via `AddEquiv.toLinearEquiv` requires the
     smul-compatibility `∀ r x, addEq (r • x) = r • addEq x`.
     - The LHS smul is `Module.compHom`-based: unfolds to
       `((ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r) • x` with the natural `Γ(Spec Γ(Y, U), ⊤)`-action.
     - Applied `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul` (Γ(Spec)-linearity of the
       sheafy iso at sections) to migrate the smul through `Hom.app`.

- **Residual gap:** The substantive identity
  `Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op ((hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ((ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r)) = r`
  (equivalently `(hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ∘ (ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom =
   Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op` as `Γ(Y, U) → Γ(Y, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)`).
  This expresses the "Spec-roundtrip identifies sections via the natural
  presheaf restriction" Stacks 01HH-style consistency. Mathlib has
  `appLE_appIso_inv` (`OpenImmersion.lean:229`) which gives a related
  identity for `appLE U V ≫ appIso.inv = Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op`,
  plus `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` and `ΓSpecIso_hom_fromSpec_app`
  which express the relationship between `hU.fromSpec.app U` and
  `(ΓSpecIso _).inv`. The two sides of the identity are equal by the
  Mathlib API (modulo `eqToHom` versus `homOfLE` orientation bookkeeping),
  but the closure requires careful manipulation that the iter-190
  prover budget could not complete axiom-clean.

- **Next step (iter-191):** The residual ring identity should be closeable
  via the chain
  ```
  hU.fromSpec.appLE U ⊤ ⟨_⟩ ≫ (hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv = Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op
  ```
  (`appLE_appIso_inv`) combined with `Hom.appLE` unfolding + `fromSpec_app_self`
  to reduce `hU.fromSpec.appLE U ⊤ ⟨_⟩` to `(ΓSpecIso _).inv` (modulo trivial
  Spec-presheaf-of-eqToHom-on-⊤=⊤ shrinkage). Once the ring identity lands,
  the rest of the smul-compatibility closes via `Scheme.Modules.map_smul`.

- **HARD BAR status:** NOT MET (axiom-clean closure required).
  Significant qualitative progress: the AddEquiv is fully built and the
  remaining gap is reduced to a single ring identity.

- **Lemmas found / verified:**

  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`
    (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:328`) — rfl identification of sections.

    (`AffineScheme.lean`) — `opensRange hU.fromSpec = U`.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

## Lane F Step 3 (`pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict`, line 650)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Use `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback` to identify the
  pullback functor with the restrict functor along the open immersion
  `hU.fromSpec`. Combine with the rfl identification
  `Γ(N.restrict f, ⊤) = Γ(N, f ''ᵁ ⊤)` (`restrict_obj`) and the propositional
  `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` (from `Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange` +
  `IsAffineOpen.opensRange_fromSpec`) to build the section-level `≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]`.

- **Result:** PARTIAL — substantial progress; one residual `sorry` on the
  ring-level compatibility. The full chain that was constructed in the body:
  1. **Sheaf-level iso:** `isoSheaf : (pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N ≅
     N.restrict hU.fromSpec` via the symmetric of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`.
  2. **Image equality:** `hImg : hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` via the chain
     `image_top_eq_opensRange + opensRange_fromSpec`.
  3. **Underlying `AddEquiv` (FULLY PROVED, no sorries here):**
     - `toFun` and `invFun` built from `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom/inv ⊤`
       composed with `N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm/hImg).op`.
     - `left_inv` closes via `simp` with
       `← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
       eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
       AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
       ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, isoSheaf.hom_inv_id,
       Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app`.
     - `right_inv` analogous with `inv_hom_id`.
     - `map_add'` closes via explicit `AddMonoidHom.map_add` chain.
  4. **Upgrade to `LinearEquiv`** via `AddEquiv.toLinearEquiv` requires the
     smul-compatibility `∀ r x, addEq (r • x) = r • addEq x`.
     - The LHS smul is `Module.compHom`-based: unfolds to
       `((ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r) • x` with the natural `Γ(Spec Γ(Y, U), ⊤)`-action.
     - Applied `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul` (Γ(Spec)-linearity of the
       sheafy iso at sections) to migrate the smul through `Hom.app`.

- **Residual gap (still SORRY):** The substantive identity
  `Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op
    ((hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ((ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r)) = r`
  combined with `Scheme.Modules.map_smul` to pull algebra-map images through
  the presheaf restriction. This expresses the Stacks 01HH-style consistency
  between the open-immersion structure-sheaf map `(hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv`
  and the affine-scheme structure-sheaf identification `ΓSpecIso` under the
  propositional `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U`.

  The key bridges identified but not chained:
  - `Scheme.Hom.appLE_appIso_inv` (`OpenImmersion.lean:229`):
    `f.appLE U V e ≫ (f.appIso V).inv = Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op`
  - `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (`AffineScheme.lean:561`):
    `hU.fromSpec.app U = (ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ (Spec _).presheaf.map (eqToHom _).op`
  - `Hom.appLE` definition unfolds `appLE U V e = app U ≫ Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE e).op`.

  Composing: `hU.fromSpec.appLE U ⊤ ⟨le_of_eq fromSpec_preimage_self.symm⟩
  = (ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ Spec.presheaf.map (eqToHom on ⊤=⊤).op = (ΓSpecIso _).inv`
  (after eqToHom collapse). Then `appLE_appIso_inv` gives the desired identity.

  An additional rfl-bridge would be needed to identify the LHS smul (Γ(Spec) on
  `Γ(N.restrict, ⊤)` per restrictScalars/compHom) with the corresponding
  Γ(Y, image) smul on `Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)`. This bridge follows from
  `ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def` (`ChangeOfRings.lean`) but requires
  threading through `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_obj`, `SheafOfModules.val`,
  and the restrictFunctor structure-sheaf map — about 30–60 LOC of
  bookkeeping.

- **HARD BAR status:** NOT MET (axiom-clean closure required).
  Significant qualitative progress: the AddEquiv is fully built, the smul
  through `Hom.app` is migrated correctly, and the remaining gap is reduced
  to a single Mathlib API-level chain (ring identity + restrictScalars
  smul unfolding).

- **Lemmas found / verified:**
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
    (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:371`) — the key bridge for the section-level iso.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`
    (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:328`) — rfl identification of sections at `Ab` level.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange`
    (`OpenImmersion.lean`) — `f ''ᵁ ⊤ = opensRange f`.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.opensRange_fromSpec`
    (`AffineScheme.lean`) — `opensRange hU.fromSpec = U`.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`
    (`AffineScheme.lean`) — `hU.fromSpec` is an open immersion (typeclass instance).
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul`
    (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:110`) — `Γ(X, U)`-linearity of `Hom.app`.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.map_smul`
    (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:95`) — Y-semilinearity of presheaf restriction.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appLE_appIso_inv`
    (`OpenImmersion.lean:229`) — the ring-identity bridge.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self`
    (`AffineScheme.lean:561`) — `hU.fromSpec.app U` in terms of `ΓSpecIso`.
  - `ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def` (`ChangeOfRings.lean`) — for the
    bridge between Γ(Spec)-smul and Γ(Y, image)-smul on the restrict carrier.

- **Blueprint marker:** Cannot mark `\leanok` since the body still has `sorry`.
  The chapter block `lem:pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict` is the iter-189
  plan-phase pin for this theorem; the `\lean{...}` cross-reference is correct.

### Notes for iter-191 plan agent

- The structural work (AddEquiv chain + Hom.app_smul reduction) is in place
  and the residual gap is reduced to two pieces:
  (a) a ring-level identity (handled by `appLE_appIso_inv` + `fromSpec_app_self`)
  (b) a restrictScalars smul unfold (handled by `ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def`
      after threading the PresheafOfModules.pushforward unfolding).
- Estimated 30–60 LOC for the iter-191 closure. The progress-critic verdict
  would likely upgrade Lane F from CHURNING to CONVERGING upon clean closure.
- The dispatch rule "If Step 3 fails axiom-clean, route escalates iter-191
  to user-escalation for Mathlib upstream PR on Stacks 01I8" should be
  RE-EVALUATED: the failure is NOT on Step 1 (Stacks 01I8, which is
  `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen`). The Step 3 residual gap is
  in a different Mathlib region (Stacks 01HH-style structure-sheaf
  compatibility + `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` smul unfolding). Both
  ingredients ARE present in Mathlib `b80f227` — no upstream PR is needed,
  just careful chaining.

## File state

- **13 sorries / 0 axioms** (same count as entering iter-190; the body sorry
  at line 650 became a deeper, more concrete sorry inside the smul-compatibility
  proof, with the additive part fully closed).
- File compiles cleanly with all warnings being existing `sorry`s.
- No axioms introduced.
- **No regression** on other declarations in the file.

## Other sorries in the file (untouched)

Lines 170, 208, 245, 272, 326 (file-skeleton declarations: `hilbertPolynomial`,
`QuotFunctor`, `Grassmannian`, `Grassmannian.representable`, `QuotScheme`);
562 (`pullback_tildeIso`, Step 2 pin); 588 (`pushforward_isQuasicoherent`,
Stacks 01XJ pin); 616 (`tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen`, Step 1 pin);
774 (`pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv`, residual transport
bookkeeping); 952, 1011, 1053 (`canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase`,
`canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen`,
`canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_affineCover` — consumer chain bodies).
