12	\section{Setup and motivation}
13	This chapter is the fourth and final sub-build chapter
14	\texttt{RR.1}--\texttt{RR.4} closing the project's headline \emph{RR bridge}
15	\[
16	  \mathtt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}
17	  \,\colon\,
18	  \text{a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with
19	  \(g(C) = 0\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).}
20	\]
21	The chain assembled across the four sub-build chapters is:
22	\begin{itemize}
23	  \item \texttt{RR.1} (\texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}): the Weil-divisor
24	    group \(\Div(C)\), the degree map
25	    \(\deg \colon \Div(C) \to \mathbb Z\) (\texttt{def:divisor_degree}), and the
26	    degree-zero principal-divisor identity
27	    (\texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero}).
28	  \item \texttt{RR.2} (\texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RRFormula}): the Riemann--Roch
29	    formula in genus zero \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\) for \(\deg(D) \geq 0\)
30	    (\texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}).
31	  \item \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter, dispatched parallel iter-174):
32	    the line bundle \(\mathcal O_C([P])\), its global-sections identification
33	    \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) = 2\), and the corollary
34	    \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} producing a non-constant
35	    rational function $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
36	    \bar k \cdot 1\( on a genus-\)0\( curve with a marked \)\bar k$-point.
37	  \item \texttt{RR.4} (\emph{this chapter}): the global classification ``a
38	    smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\)
39	    and a \(\bar k\)-rational point is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).''
40	\end{itemize}
41	This chapter consumes \texttt{RR.3} (the non-constant function) and produces
42	the Lean target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} pinned at
43	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean} line~290 and restated as
44	the interface block \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} of
45	\texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex}. The \texttt{RR.4}-closure of that target
46	also drops the \texttt{sorryAx} propagation tagged on
47	\texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme} via
48	\texttt{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}.
49	\paragraph{Scope of the \(\bar k\)-base classification.} The chapter restricts
50	to the case where the base field \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed. Over $\bar
51	k\( every closed point is \)\bar k\(-rational, so the ``\)C\( has a \)\bar
52	k$-rational point'' hypothesis of Hartshorne's Exercise~IV.1.3 is automatic
53	(the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) is non-empty,
54	hence has closed points, all of which are \(\bar k\)-rational). The general
55	``\(k \to \bar k\) descent'' step ``genus-\(0\) curve over a non-closed \(k\) with
56	a \(k\)-rational point \(\cong \mathbb P^1_k\)'' is a separate sub-build hosted
57	in \texttt{Jacobian.tex} (the \texttt{genusZeroWitness.key} downstream
58	consumer) and is out of scope here.
59	\paragraph{Standing hypotheses.} Throughout this chapter, \(\bar k\) is an
60	algebraically closed field and \(C\) is a smooth proper geometrically
61	irreducible curve over \(\bar k\). The Lean signature
62	(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean}) threads the
63	project's standing curve typeclass package:
64	\begin{quote}
65	  \texttt{\{kbar : Type u\} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]\\
66	   (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]\\
67	   \phantom{}\quad[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]\\
68	   \phantom{}\quad[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]}
69	\end{quote}
70	matching the typeclass set used by the headline target
71	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} in
72	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290--296}. The genus
73	\(g(C)\) is the one defined in \texttt{def:genus} of \texttt{Genus.tex},
74	namely \(g(C) = \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)\) via the project's
75	\(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured sheaf cohomology
76	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genus}); the concrete projective line target
77	\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is the project's
78	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar~kbar} of
79	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean}.
80	\section{The morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from two global sections}
81	\begin{lemma}
82	\leanok
83	[Morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from a globally generating pair of sections]
84	  \label{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}
85	  
86	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}
87	  Let \(X\) be a scheme over a field \(\bar k\), let \(\mathcal L\) be an invertible
88	  sheaf on \(X\), and let \(s_0, s_1 \in H^0(X, \mathcal L)\) be two global
89	  sections whose common zero locus is empty:
90	  \[
91	    \{x \in X : s_0(x) = 0 \text{ and } s_1(x) = 0\} \;=\; \emptyset.
92	  \]
93	  (Equivalently, \(s_0\) and \(s_1\) generate \(\mathcal L\) at every point.) Then
94	  there exists a unique morphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes
95	  \[
96	    \varphi_{(s_0, s_1)} \colon X \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
97	  \]
98	  whose pullback of \(\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(1)\) is \(\mathcal L\) and whose
99	  pullbacks of the standard coordinate sections $x_0, x_1 \in
100	  H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal O(1))\( are \)s_0\( and \)s_1$ respectively. On the
101	  basic-open chart $D_+(x_0) = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k} \subset
102	  \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\( the morphism \)\varphi_{(s_0, s_1)}\( is the map ``\)x
103	  \mapsto s_1(x) / s_0(x)\(''; on the chart \)D_+(x_1)\( it is the map ``\)x
104	  \mapsto s_0(x) / s_1(x)$''; the two definitions agree on the overlap
105	  \(D_+(x_0) \cap D_+(x_1) = \mathbb G_{m, \bar k}\) by the cocycle identity
106	  \((s_1 / s_0) \cdot (s_0 / s_1) = 1\).
107	\end{lemma}
108	\begin{proof}
109	  This is the standard linear-system construction in the form of
110	  Hartshorne's Theorem~II.7.1 (``Morphisms to \(\mathbb P^n\)''), specialised to
111	  \(n = 1\). Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) ships the corresponding API
112	  under the name
113	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} in
114	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}:
115	  \begin{quote}
116	  \small
117	  \texttt{def Proj.fromOfGlobalSections \{\(\sigma\) A\} [CommRing A]
118	    [SetLike \(\sigma\) A] [AddSubgroupClass \(\sigma\) A] (\(\mathcal A\) :
119	    \(\mathbb N\) → \(\sigma\)) [GradedRing \(\mathcal A\)] \{X : Scheme\}\\
120	    \phantom{}\quad(f : A →+* X.presheaf.obj \(\top\))\\
121	    \phantom{}\quad(hf : Ideal.map f (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant \(\mathcal A\)).toIdeal = \(\top\)) :\\
122	    \phantom{}\quad(X \(\longrightarrow\) Proj \(\mathcal A\))}
123	  \end{quote}
124	  Specialising to \(\mathcal A = \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) with the standard $\mathbb
125	  N\(-grading by total degree, the inputs are a graded \)\bar k$-algebra
126	  homomorphism \(f \colon \bar k[x_0, x_1] \to H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) and a
127	  proof that the irrelevant ideal \((x_0, x_1) \cdot \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) maps to
128	  the unit ideal of \(H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) (i.e.\ no common zeros). The
129	  resulting morphism lands in $\mathrm{Proj}\,\bar k[x_0, x_1] = \mathbb
130	  P^1_{\bar k}\( (Hartshorne~II, Example~7.1.3, the construction of \)\mathbb
131	  P^n\( as a \)\mathrm{Proj}\(). When \)\mathcal L$ is non-trivial the present
132	  lemma's signature is reduced to the Mathlib API by working on the open
133	  cover \(X = D(s_0) \cup D(s_1)\), where on each chart the sections become
134	  honest global functions (\(s_1 / s_0\) on \(D(s_0)\), \(s_0 / s_1\) on \(D(s_1)\),
135	  agreeing on the overlap). The chart-glue is performed by the
136	  project-bespoke wrapper
137	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}, whose body
138	  in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} chains
139	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} with the chart-restriction-and-glue
140	  pattern of \texttt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}.
141	  Uniqueness follows from the standard separation argument for morphisms to
142	  a separated scheme: two morphisms \(\varphi, \psi \colon X \to \mathbb P^1\)
143	  with \(\varphi^* x_i = \psi^* x_i\) for \(i = 0, 1\) agree on the basic-open
144	  charts \(D_+(x_0), D_+(x_1)\) (where they pull back the affine coordinates to
145	  the same rational function), and these charts cover \(\mathbb P^1\), so the
146	  two morphisms agree globally by gluing.
147	\end{proof}
148	\section{Degree of a morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) via its pole divisor}
149	% NOTE (iter-190 plan-phase Pin 2 corrective; supersedes iter-182 narrative).
150	% `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor \varphi` is the Weil divisor \(\varphi^*[\infty]\),
151	% defined as `positivePart (principal (\varphi^{\#} t_\infty))` in Lean.
152	% The supporting `Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` def lives in
153	% `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` (\ref{def:WeilDivisor_positivePart}); the
154	% Hartshorne~II.6.9 identity that powers the existential degree statement
155	% lives there as \ref{lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank}.
156	\begin{lemma}
157	\leanok
158	[Degree of a non-constant morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) via the preimage of \(\infty\)]
159	  \label{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor}
160	  
161	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor}
162	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV \S2, opening paragraph of "Hurwitz's Theorem"
163	  % (definition of the degree of a finite morphism of curves) (read from
164	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
165	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
166	  % \(f: X \to Y\) of curves is defined to be the degree \([K(X):K(Y)]\) of the
167	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
168	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.2, p.~299, opening of \S2.}
169	  Let \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) be a non-constant morphism
170	  from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\). Then
171	  \(\varphi\) is finite, its function-field extension
172	  \(\bar k(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)\) (the canonical
173	  \(\varphi\)-induced map, supplied as an
174	  \texttt{[Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField C.functionField]}
175	  instance argument on the Lean side) is finite, and there exists a Weil
176	  divisor \(D \in \Div(C)\) such that
177	  \[
178	    D \;=\; \varphi^*[\infty]
179	    \quad\text{and}\quad
180	    \deg(D) \;=\; [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,].
181	  \]
182	  Equivalently, the morphism \(\varphi\) satisfies
183	  \[
184	    [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,] \;=\;
185	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
186	                  \ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [P]\,\right),
187	  \]
188	  where \(t_\infty \in K(\mathbb P^1)\) is a local parameter at \(\infty\)
189	  (equivalently \(t_\infty = 1/u\) for the standard affine coordinate \(u\) on
190	  \(\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}\)); and for any closed point
191	  \(Q \in \mathbb P^1\) the same identification gives
192	  \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C):\bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\).
193	  \emph{Convention on the pole divisor.} The Weil divisor
194	  \(\varphi^*[\infty]\) is exposed in Lean as
195	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor \(\varphi\)}, a
196	  noncomputable definition introduced (as a typed \texttt{sorry}) at the
197	  iter-182 plan-phase signature strengthening of this lemma. The body of
198	  \texttt{poleDivisor} is queued for iter-183+ closure via the standard
199	  affine-chart construction (\texttt{Ideal.sum\_ramification\_inertia}
200	  in Mathlib, applied at each closed point \(P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)\)
201	  to read off the local ramification multiplicity
202	  \(\ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty)\) from the maximal ideal at \(\infty\) in
203	  \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)). On the Lean side, the existential output of
204	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} packages
205	  \(D = \texttt{Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor}\;\varphi\) together with
206	  \(\deg(D) = \operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(\mathbb P^1)} K(C)\),
207	  mirroring the OCofP pattern in which a typed-sorry def
208	  (\texttt{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_toFunctionField}) is introduced
209	  upstream of its consumer lemma.
210	\end{lemma}
211	% NOTE (iter-190 plan-phase, supersedes iter-189 review NOTE):
212	% Pin 2 corrective COMMITTED — Option (a). `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` is
213	% being refactored to return the *positive part* of the principal divisor
214	% of `\varphi^{\#} t_\infty`, i.e. `(div(\varphi^{\#} t_\infty))_0
215	% = \varphi^*[\infty]`. The supporting Weil-divisor `positivePart`
216	% definition lives in `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` as
217	% `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart`; the substantive Hartshorne~II.6.9
218	% identity that powers `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` is now pinned as
219	% `lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` in `WeilDivisor.tex`.
220	% With these substrates in place, `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` is
221	% mathematically correct as stated: the body's existential witness `D`
222	% is `Hom.poleDivisor \varphi = positivePart (principal (\varphi^{\#}
223	% t_\infty))`, and the degree identity is a one-line corollary of
224	% `lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`. iter-190 prover lane
225	% (Lane I) implements the refactor:
226	%   1. New def `Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` in `WeilDivisor.lean`
227	%      (~10-15 LOC; lattice `D \sqcup 0`).
228	%   2. New lemma
229	%      `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` as a
230	%      named typed-sorry pin in `WeilDivisor.lean` (~5 LOC signature; body
231	%      ~50-80 LOC via affine-chart `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` route
232	%      owed iter-191+).
233	%   3. Refactor `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` body in `RationalCurveIso.lean`
234	%      from `principal (algebraMap _ _ t.val) halg` to
235	%      `positivePart (principal (algebraMap _ _ t.val) halg)` (~5 LOC).
236	%   4. Close `Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` body via a 1-line
237	%      rewrite consuming (2) (~3 LOC).
238	% Net iter-190 Lane I: 1 axiom-clean closure (Pin 2 body, modulo named
239	% typed sorry on (2)); 1 NEW named typed-sorry pin
240	% (`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`) which is itself an
241	% iter-191+ Hartshorne II.6.9 substrate target. See iter-190 sidecar
242	% `iter/iter-190/plan.md` for the full rationale.
243	\begin{proof}
244	  \uses{def:WeilDivisor_positivePart, lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank, def:principal_divisor, def:divisor_degree, thm:principal_deg_zero}
245	  A non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves over \(\bar k\) is
246	  automatically finite. Indeed, the image of \(\varphi\) is a closed
247	  irreducible subset of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) that is not a point (else
248	  \(\varphi\) would be constant) and not empty, so it is all of $\mathbb
249	  P^1_{\bar k}\( (the only proper irreducible closed subset of \)\mathbb
250	  P^1_{\bar k}\( is a point); hence \)\varphi$ is surjective. A surjective
251	  morphism between smooth proper irreducible curves of equal dimension is
252	  finite (proper + quasi-finite, where quasi-finiteness comes from the
253	  fact that \(\varphi\) has zero-dimensional fibres: a positive-dimensional
254	  fibre would force a one-dimensional irreducible component in the fibre,
255	  contradicting irreducibility of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) and \(C\) at
256	  generic points). Hence the function-field extension
257	  \(\bar k(\mathbb P^1) = \varphi^\# K(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)\)
258	  is finite.
259	  The identification \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\) for
260	  any closed point \(Q \in \mathbb P^1\) is the multiplicativity of the
261	  degree under finite pullback (Hartshorne~II.6.9): for a finite morphism
262	  \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) of smooth proper curves and any divisor $D \in
263	  \Div(C')\(, \)\deg(\varphi^* D) = \deg(\varphi) \cdot \deg(D)$. Specialising
264	  to \(D = [Q]\) (a divisor of degree \(1\) since \(\kappa(Q) = \bar k\)) gives
265	  \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = \deg(\varphi)\). Equivalently, the degree of
266	  \(\varphi\) equals the number of preimages of any closed point of $\mathbb
267	  P^1\(, counted with the ramification multiplicities \)\ord_P(\varphi^*
268	  t_\infty)\( at the preimage points \)P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)$.
269	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The lemma threads two auxiliary inputs:
270	  ``non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is finite'' (the
271	  surjective-and-proper-and-quasi-finite argument above; Hartshorne~II.6.8)
272	  and ``multiplicativity of degree under finite pullback'' (Hartshorne~II.6.9
273	  and the multiplicativity of \(\deg\) under composition of \(\div\) and
274	  \(\deg\); the closely related ``\(\mathrm{div}(f)\) has degree zero'' specialisation
275	  is \texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero} of \texttt{RR.1}). Both are separable
276	  sub-lemmas inside \texttt{RR.4}'s Lean file
277	  (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean}) and are
278	  slotted for closure in a follow-up iteration after the
279	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} skeleton lands.
280	\end{proof}
281	\section{A degree-\(1\) morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism}
282	% NOTE (iter-181 Lane I signature refinement; see analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md
283	% Decision 2, verdict DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY). The iter-177 file-skeleton
284	% placeholder hypothesis "Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)"
285	% (an abstract function-field iso, unrelated to \(\varphi\)) was strictly
286	% weaker than the birational-extension body needs and was refined in
287	% iter-181 to the explicit function-field-extension-degree formulation
288	% [K(C):K(C')] = 1, i.e. Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField
289	% = 1, with C.functionField a C'.functionField-algebra via the canonical
290	% \varphi-induced map (Scheme.Hom.stalkMap at the generic point composed
291	% with IsFractionRing.lift). The proof-prose body below (the Hartshorne
292	% I.6.12 chain + scheme-theoretic alternative argument) is unchanged ---
293	% only the input shape changes.
294	\begin{lemma}
295	\leanok
296	[Degree-\(1\) morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism]
297	  \label{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso}
298	  
299	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one}
300	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], I \S6 Corollary~6.12 (function-field-to-curve
301	  % equivalence of categories) (read from
302	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 62, doc p.~45)
303	  % + [Hartshorne], IV \S2 opening paragraph (degree of a finite morphism
304	  % of curves is the function-field extension degree) (read from
305	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
306	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Corollary 6.12. \emph{The following three categories are
307	  % equivalent:}
308	  % (i) \emph{nonsingular projective curves, and dominant morphisms;}
309	  % (ii) \emph{quasi-projective curves, and dominant rational maps;}
310	  % (iii) \emph{function fields of dimension 1 over \(k\), and \(k\)-homomorphisms.}"
311	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
312	  % \(f: X \to Y\) of curves is defined to be the degree \([K(X):K(Y)]\) of the
313	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
314	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, I.6 Corollary~6.12 (p.~45) + IV.2 opening
315	  (p.~299).}
316	  Let \(C, C'\) be smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves over an
317	  algebraically closed field \(\bar k\), and let \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) be
318	  a non-constant morphism whose induced function-field extension is of
319	  degree one,
320	  \[
321	    [\,K(C) : K(C')\,] \;=\; 1,
322	  \]
323	  formally
324	  \(\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(C')} K(C) = 1\) in the Lean signature.
325	  Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes.
326	  \emph{Convention on the function-field map.} At each call-site of this
327	  lemma, the \(K(C')\)-algebra structure on \(K(C)\) used to interpret the
328	  degree \([K(C) : K(C')]\) is the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced
329	  function-field map, i.e.\ the composite
330	  \[
331	    K(C') \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{germ}\;}\;
332	    \mathcal O_{C', \varphi(\xi_C)}
333	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\varphi^\#\;}\;
334	    \mathcal O_{C, \xi_C}
335	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{frac.}\,\text{lift}\;}\; K(C)
336	  \]
337	  of the germ at the image of the generic point \(\xi_C \in C\) with the
338	  stalk-map of \(\varphi\) at \(\xi_C\) and the fraction-field lift at
339	  \(\xi_C\) (per \texttt{analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md}, Decision~2). On
340	  the Lean side this canonical instance is carried as a
341	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
342	  to \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one}; consumers
343	  supply it explicitly at each call-site.
344	\end{lemma}
345	% NOTE (iter-189 review): Pin 3 Step 1 (function-field iso K(C') ≃+* K(C)
346	% from `Module.finrank = 1`) closed axiom-clean inline iter-189 (~10 LOC via
347	% `Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_of_finrank_eq_one` + `Algebra.surjective_algebraMap_iff`
348	% + `RingHom.injective` + `RingEquiv.ofBijective`). Step 2 (scheme-level lift
349	% via `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization`) remains a typed sorry in the body, with 4
350	% sub-obligations (a)-(d) documented inline at the sorry. Iter-190+ closure
351	% budget ~80-150 LOC per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`.
352	\begin{proof}
353	  The function-field hypothesis \([K(C) : K(C')] = 1\) states that the
354	  canonical \(\varphi\)-induced map \(\varphi^\# \colon K(C') \to K(C)\) is a
355	  finite extension of degree one; since \(K(C')\) is a field and the
356	  extension has dimension one as a \(K(C')\)-vector space, \(\varphi^\#\) is
357	  an isomorphism of function fields \(K(C') \cong K(C)\). (Equivalently, by
358	  the IV.2-opening identification \(\deg(\varphi) = [K(C):K(C')]\), this is
359	  the statement \(\deg(\varphi) = 1\); the morphism \(\varphi\) is finite in
360	  this case by the standard argument that a non-constant morphism of
361	  smooth proper curves is finite, identical to the
362	  \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} preamble.)
363	  By Hartshorne's Corollary~I.6.12, the assignment \(C \mapsto \bar k(C)\) is
364	  an equivalence of categories between smooth projective curves over $\bar
365	  k$ (with dominant morphisms) and function fields of transcendence degree
366	  one over \(\bar k\) (with \(\bar k\)-homomorphisms). Under this equivalence,
367	  the isomorphism \(\bar k(C') \cong \bar k(C)\) produced by the previous
368	  paragraph lifts to a unique isomorphism of smooth proper curves $C \to
369	  C'\(, and this isomorphism agrees with \)\varphi$ on the generic point (by
370	  construction: it is the inverse-image of \(\varphi^\#\) under the
371	  equivalence). The two morphisms \(C \to C'\) that agree on the generic
372	  point of the integral scheme \(C\) agree everywhere (a morphism out of an
373	  integral scheme to a separated scheme is determined by its restriction
374	  to the generic point, Hartshorne~II Ex.~4.2). Hence \(\varphi\) is itself
375	  the lift, and is therefore an isomorphism.
376	  \emph{Alternative direct argument.} Without invoking the
377	  equivalence-of-categories framing, the same conclusion is obtained
378	  scheme-theoretically: a finite degree-\(1\) morphism $\varphi \colon C \to
379	  C'$ between integral normal noetherian schemes is an isomorphism.
380	  Indeed, the direct image \(\varphi_* \mathcal O_C\) is a coherent
381	  \(\mathcal O_{C'}\)-module that is generically of rank $\deg(\varphi) =
382	  1\(, and an inclusion \)\mathcal O_{C'} \hookrightarrow \varphi_*
383	  \mathcal O_C\( of coherent rank-\)1$ sheaves whose generic ranks agree
384	  is an isomorphism over the locus where the quotient
385	  \(\varphi_* \mathcal O_C / \mathcal O_{C'}\) has trivial stalk. The
386	  quotient is a torsion coherent \(\mathcal O_{C'}\)-module of generic rank
387	  \(\deg(\varphi) - 1 = 0\), hence is itself torsion-free (a
388	  finitely-generated torsion module over a Dedekind domain of generic
389	  rank zero is zero), so vanishes. Therefore $\varphi_* \mathcal O_C =
390	  \mathcal O_{C'}\( and \)\varphi$ is the structure morphism of an
391	  isomorphism. Cf.\ Stacks tag~\textbf{0AVX} for the categorical version.
392	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The Lean signature
393	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one} consumes a
394	  non-constant morphism \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) together with a
395	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
396	  (the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced function-field map) and the
397	  function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis
398	  \texttt{Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1}, and
399	  produces an isomorphism \(C \cong C'\) in the slice category
400	  \(\mathtt{Over (Spec (.of\ kbar))}\). The proof body threads the Mathlib
401	  API \texttt{IsIntegralHom.iso\_of\_isUnit} (or the equivalent
402	  \texttt{IsAffineHom + Finite + RankOne} chain) and, where needed, the
403	  project-bespoke ``finite-morphism-of-degree-one is an isomorphism''
404	  helper. The Hartshorne~I.6.12 equivalence-of-categories framing is the
405	  intended high-level proof structure; the scheme-theoretic argument
406	  above is the spelled-out form for the Lean body.
407	\end{proof}
408	\section{Headline: genus-\(0\) curve over \(\bar k\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1\)}
409	\begin{theorem}
410	
411	\leanok
412	[Genus-\(0\) curve over \(\bar k\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1\)]
413	  \label{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}
414	  
415	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
416	  % already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) (I, Ex. 7.2), so conversely
417	  % suppose given a curve \(X\) of genus 0. Let \(P, Q\) be two distinct
418	  % points of \(X\) and apply Riemann-Roch to the divisor \(D = P - Q\).
419	  % Since \(\deg(K - D) = -2\), using (1.3.3) above, we have \(l(K - D) = 0\),
420	  % and so we find \(l(D) = 1\). But \(D\) is a divisor of degree 0, so by
421	  % (1.2) we have \(D \sim 0\), in other words \(P \sim Q\). But this implies
422	  % \(X\) is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
423	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Example~1.3.5, p.~297.}
424	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, and let \(C\) be a smooth
425	  proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\).
426	  Then there exists an isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes
427	  \[
428	    \varphi \colon C \stackrel{\sim}{\longrightarrow} \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}.
429	  \]
430	  Concretely, the Lean signature
431	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} (the existing pinned
432	  target at \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290}) is
433	  closed by the proof below; the existing interface block
434	  \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} of
435	  \texttt{chap:avr_for_rr} is its consumer-side restatement,
436	  pinning the same Lean declaration.
437	\end{theorem}
438	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the verbatim Example IV.1.3.5 text in the
439	% statement block above (Hartshorne states the classification and its
440	% proof inside the single Example; the proof is the second half beginning
441	% "We already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) \ldots"). The present
442	% proof body restates Hartshorne's argument in the project's notation
443	% (linear-system construction \(C \to \mathbb P^1\) via $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C
444	% ([P]))\(, degree-\)1$ identification, isomorphism).
445	\begin{proof}
446	  Over the algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) the smooth proper
447	  geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) has closed points, all of which are
448	  \(\bar k\)-rational (the residue field of a closed point of a curve over
449	  \(\bar k\) is a finite extension of \(\bar k\), hence is \(\bar k\) itself by
450	  algebraic closedness). Fix one such \(\bar k\)-rational point \(P \in C\);
451	  this is the marked point used to build the linear system below.
452	  \emph{Step 1: non-constant function \(f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\).}
453	  By Hartshorne's Riemann--Roch in genus zero
454	  (\texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} of \texttt{RR.2}) applied to the
455	  effective divisor \([P]\) of degree \(1\), we obtain
456	  \(\ell([P]) = \deg([P]) + 1 = 2\). The constant section
457	  \(1 \in \bar k \cdot 1 \subset H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\) accounts for a
458	  one-dimensional \(\bar k\)-subspace; the two-dimensional quotient supplies
459	  a non-constant section $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
460	  \bar k \cdot 1$. This is the corollary
461	  \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling
462	  chapter, \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OCofP.tex}).
463	  \emph{Step 2: morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1\) via the linear system \((1, f)\).}
464	  The pair \((1, f) \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))^2\) generates the
465	  invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) at every point of \(C\): at points
466	  \(Q \in C \setminus \{P\}\) the section \(1\) does not vanish in
467	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])_Q \cong \mathcal O_{C, Q}\), and at the marked point
468	  \(P\) the section \(f\) does not vanish in \(\mathcal O_C([P])_P\) (because \(f\)
469	  has a pole of order exactly \(1\) at \(P\) in the rational-function
470	  picture: \(f \in K(C) \setminus \bar k\) with \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = [P]\)).
471	  Therefore by \texttt{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections} there
472	  exists a unique morphism
473	  \[
474	    \varphi \;:=\; \varphi_{(1, f)} \colon C \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
475	  \]
476	  with \(\varphi^* x_0 = 1\) and \(\varphi^* x_1 = f\) (so the pullback of
477	  the standard ratio coordinate \(u = x_1 / x_0\) on $\mathbb P^1 \setminus
478	  \{\infty\}\( is \)f$, and the pullback of the inverse coordinate
479	  \(t_\infty = x_0 / x_1 = 1 / f\) is the local parameter \(1/f\) at \(P\)).
480	  \emph{Step 3: \(\deg(\varphi) = 1\) via the pole divisor of \(f\).} The
481	  morphism \(\varphi\) is non-constant (since \(f\) is non-constant as a
482	  rational function on \(C\), the pullback \(\varphi^* u = f\) takes
483	  infinitely many values, ruling out a constant \(\varphi\)). By
484	  \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} applied to \(\varphi\),
485	  \[
486	    \deg(\varphi)
487	    \;=\;
488	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{Q \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
489	                  \ord_Q(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [Q]\,\right).
490	  \]
491	  We compute the pole divisor of \(f\) as a rational function on \(C\):
492	  since \(f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\) with \(f\) non-constant and
493	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) the line bundle of the effective divisor \([P]\), the
494	  rational-function \(f\) has pole divisor \((f)_\infty = [P]\) (the
495	  unique pole of order \(1\) at \(P\), no other poles; this is the standard
496	  identification of \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))\) with the rational functions
497	  \(g \in K(C)\) satisfying \(\mathrm{div}(g) + D \geq 0\), specialised to
498	  \(D = [P]\)). Hence \(\varphi^{-1}(\infty) = \{P\}\) with multiplicity~\(1\)
499	  and
500	  \[
501	    \deg(\varphi) \;=\; \deg([P]) \;=\; 1.
502	  \]
503	  \emph{Step 4: \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism.} By Hartshorne's IV.2
504	  identification \(\deg(\varphi) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\), the
505	  degree-one conclusion of Step~3 rewrites as
506	  \(\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{\bar k(\mathbb P^1)} K(C) = 1\) (with
507	  the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced
508	  \texttt{[Algebra \,(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField\,
509	   C.functionField]} instance). By
510	  \texttt{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso} applied to the non-constant
511	  morphism \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) (smooth proper
512	  geometrically irreducible curves over \(\bar k\) on both sides), under
513	  this function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis, \(\varphi\) is an
514	  isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes.
515	  This produces the required isomorphism \(C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)
516	  closing the headline target
517	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}.
518	  \emph{Comparison with Hartshorne's original argument.} Hartshorne's
519	  Example~IV.1.3.5 phrases the argument in terms of two distinct points
520	  \(P, Q \in C\) and the degree-zero divisor \(D = P - Q\), deriving \(P \sim Q\)
521	  and concluding via II.6.10.1 that \(C\) is rational; combined with I.6.12
522	  this yields \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\). The present proof restates the same
523	  combinatorial content via the more directly formalisable linear-system
524	  construction: instead of going through the \(P \sim Q\) statement and
525	  invoking II.6.10.1 as a black box, we produce the explicit morphism
526	  \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1\) from the two sections \((1, f)\) of
527	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) given by Riemann--Roch and identify its degree
528	  directly. The two arguments are mathematically equivalent and have the
529	  same dependency footprint (Riemann--Roch in genus zero \(\Rightarrow\)
530	  \(\ell([P]) = 2\) \(\Rightarrow\) non-constant \(f\) \(\Rightarrow\) degree-\(1\)
531	  map to \(\mathbb P^1\) \(\Rightarrow\) iso); the linear-system phrasing maps
532	  more cleanly onto the Mathlib \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} API and
533	  the project's existing Weil-divisor/degree pipeline of \texttt{RR.1}.
534	\end{proof}
535	\begin{remark}
536	[Drop of \texttt{sorryAx} on the genus-\(0\) Albanese keystone]
537	  \label{rmk:rr4_unblocks_sorryAx_on_rigidity_genus0}
538	  Closure of \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} retires the
539	  \texttt{sorryAx} propagation tagged on
540	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme}
541	  (\texttt{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of
542	  \texttt{chap:avr_for_rr}, body landed iter-166), whose
543	  \emph{first} of two upstream \texttt{sorry} sources is the
544	  \texttt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} target this chapter closes. The
545	  \emph{second} upstream is the helper
546	  \texttt{morphism\_P1\_to\_grpScheme\_const}'s residual sorries, tracked

548	\end{remark}
549	\section{Out of scope}
550	The following items are deliberately \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.4} and
551	are sequenced elsewhere:
552	\begin{itemize}
553	  \item The general ``smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) of
554	    genus \(0\) over a \emph{non-closed} field \(k\) with a \(k\)-rational point
555	    \(\cong \mathbb P^1_k\)'' statement is the \(\bar k \to k\) descent of
556	    \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso}. The descent is hosted in
557	    \texttt{Jacobian.tex} via \texttt{genusZeroWitness.key}; it consumes
558	    \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} together with Galois descent for
559	    smooth proper curves and is out of scope for \texttt{RR.4}.
560	  \item The Mathlib API \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections}
561	    (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}) is
562	    consumed as a black box by
563	    \texttt{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}; its own proof inside
564	    Mathlib (the universal property of \(\mathrm{Proj}\), graded-ring
565	    presentation of \(\mathbb P^n\)) is not re-derived here.
566	  \item Multiplicativity of degree under finite pullback (Hartshorne~II.6.9)
567	    is consumed as a black box by \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor};
568	    its proof is a separable sub-lemma threaded inside
569	    \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} and is
570	    \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.4}'s blueprint statement (it lives at
571	    the same logical level as the closely related
572	    \texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero} of \texttt{RR.1}).
573	  \item The full equivalence of categories ``smooth projective curves over
574	    \(\bar k\) with dominant morphisms'' \(\cong\) ``function fields of
575	    transcendence degree~\(1\) over \(\bar k\) with \(\bar k\)-homomorphisms''
576	    (Hartshorne~I.6.12) is consumed as a high-level framing in
577	    \texttt{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso} but is \emph{not} re-formalised at
578	    the equivalence-of-categories level here. The proof body in the Lean
579	    file threads the spelled-out scheme-theoretic ``finite-degree-one
580	    morphism between integral normal schemes is an isomorphism''
581	    argument (Stacks~\textbf{0AVX}), which is the minimal-Mathlib-dependence
582	    pin compatible with the chapter's downstream needs.
583	  \item ``A smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\)
584	    has at least two distinct \(\bar k\)-points'' (the non-empty / infinite
585	    cardinality of \(C(\bar k)\)) is consumed implicitly by the present
586	    chapter but its formal statement lives in the project's curve API
587	    (\texttt{Genus.tex} / \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian.Genus}); the chapter
588	    does not restate it.
589	  \item Cohomology of the line bundle \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) on a curve
590	    (Hartshorne~III.5 + IV.1) is fully consumed via \texttt{RR.3}; in
591	    particular the \(H^1\)-vanishing of \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) on a genus-\(0\)
592	    curve is part of \texttt{RR.3}'s payload feeding
593	    \texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} of \texttt{RR.2}, and is not
594	    re-derived here.
595	\end{itemize}

10	# Weil divisors on a smooth proper curve (RR.1)
11	
12	This file is the **RR.1** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
13	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
14	isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
15

17	(`MeromorphicOn.divisor`, `CommRing.Pic`, `Scheme.RationalMap`) cover different
18	ground. This file is therefore **project-bespoke**, scaffolding the formal-sum data
19	type `Div(X) = ⨁_{Y ⊂ X codim 1} ℤ` on a Noetherian integral scheme `X` satisfying
20	Hartshorne's condition `(*)`, the principal-divisor homomorphism
21	`div : K(X)^× → Div(X)` on a curve, the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ` on a smooth

23	on a complete nonsingular curve (Hartshorne Cor. II.6.10), and the linear-equivalence
24	relation `D ~ D'`.

62	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
63	
64	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
65	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
66	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
67	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
68	`K(X)`.
69	
70	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
71	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
72	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
73	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
74	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
75	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
76	-/
77	
78	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
79	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
80	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
81	
82	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
83	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
84	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
85	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
86	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
87	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
88	integrality field is needed.
89	
90	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
91	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
92	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
93	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
94	  point : X
95	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
96	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
97	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
98	
99	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
100	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
101	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
102	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
105	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
106	
107	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
108	
109	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
110	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
111	
112	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
113	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
114	
115	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
116	
117	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
118	
119	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
120	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
121	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
122	
123	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
124	
125	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
126	
127	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
128	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
129	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
130	discrete valuation on `f`.
131	
132	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
133	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
134	
135	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
136	
137	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
138	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
139	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
140	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
141	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
142	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
143	
144	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
145	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
146	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
147	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
148	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
149	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
150	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
151	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
152	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
153	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
154	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
155	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
156	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
157	
158	end Scheme.RationalMap

160	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
161	
162	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
163	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
164	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
165	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
166	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
167	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
168	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
169	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
170	
171	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
172	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk
173	`O_{X,Y.point}`. The `[IsIntegral X]` precondition makes
174	`IsDomain (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` available so that the
175	`IsDiscreteValuationRing` predicate is well-formed
176	(`AlgebraicGeometry.instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral`). -/
177	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) [IsIntegral X] : Prop where
178	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk is a discrete valuation
179	  ring. (This is the precise content of Hartshorne's `(*)`; the weaker
180	  `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` derives via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹
181	  `IsPrincipalIdealRing` ⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain via the bridge
182	  instance below.) -/
183	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
184	
185	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
186	synthesis can derive `IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for
187	every prime divisor `Y`. -/
188	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instIsDiscreteValuationRingStalk
189	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
190	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
191	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
192	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
193	
194	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
195	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
196	prime divisor `Y`, via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹ `IsPrincipalIdealRing`
197	⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain (Mathlib's `IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing`
198	+ `IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one`). -/
199	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
200	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
201	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
202	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
203	  haveI : IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
204	    Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
205	  haveI : IsPrincipalIdealRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
206	    IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing
207	  IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one _
208	
209	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
210	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
211	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
212	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.
213	
214	iter-177 status: this packages Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, which Mathlib does not
215	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a
216	nonzero element `f ∈ K(X)^×` of a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely
217	many height-one primes can divide either numerator or denominator); the proof
218	factors through `IsLocallyNoetherian X` + the principal-ideal generation of
219	height-1 primes + the finite irreducible-component decomposition of
220	`V(f₀) ∪ V(f∞)`. The chapter pins this as a separate sub-build deferral
221	(`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5).
222	
223	The statement is generic in `f` (no `f ≠ 0` hypothesis is threaded): on
224	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
225	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
226	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
227	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
228	    (f : X.functionField) :
229	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
230	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by
231	  sorry

233	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
234	
235	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
236	
237	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
238	
239	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
240	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
241	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
242	
243	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
244	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
245	
246	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
247	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
248	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
249	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
250	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
251	
252	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
253	
254	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
255	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
256	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
257	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
258	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
259	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
260	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
261	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
262	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
263	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
264	hypothesised regime. -/
265	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
266	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
267	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
268	
269	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
270	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
271	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
272	multiplicity one. -/
273	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
274	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
275	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
276	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
277	
278	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
279	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
280	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
281	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
282	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
283	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
284	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
285	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
286	
287	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
288	
289	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
290	
291	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
292	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
293	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
294	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
295	
296	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
297	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
298	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
299	
300	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
301	
302	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
303	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
304	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
305	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
306	
307	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
308	
309	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
310	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
311	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
312	
313	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
314	
315	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
316	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
317	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
318	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
319	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
320	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
321	
322	@[simp]
323	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
324	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
325	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
326	
327	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
328	
329	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
330	
331	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
332	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
333	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
334	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
335	
336	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
337	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
338	
339	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
340	following definition, p. 131).
341	
342	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
343	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
344	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
345	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
346	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
347	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
348	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
349	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
350	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
351	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
352	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
353	
354	/-- **The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism `K(X)^× → Div(X)`.**
355	
356	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
357	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`
358	(equivalently: an additive map `(K(X)^×, ·) → (Div(X), +)`).
359	
360	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_hom` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131).
361	
362	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities
363	`v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`. The per-`Y` identities live in
364	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
365	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
366	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
367	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
368	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
369	  toFun u :=
370	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))
371	  map_one' := by
372	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
373	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
374	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),
375	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
376	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
377	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
378	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
379	    congr 1
380	    apply Finsupp.ext
381	    intro Y
382	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
383	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
384	    rw [Units.val_one]
385	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
386	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
387	  map_mul' u v := by
388	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
389	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
390	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
391	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
392	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
393	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
394	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)
395	    -- and `WithZero.log_mul` (the nonzero hypotheses come from
396	    -- `Units.ne_zero` and `map_ne_zero`).
397	    rw [← ofAdd_add]
398	    congr 1
399	    apply Finsupp.ext
400	    intro Y
401	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((↑(u * v) : X.functionField))
402	      = Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑u : X.functionField)
403	      + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑v : X.functionField)
404	    rw [Units.val_mul]
405	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
406	    rw [map_mul]
407	    exact WithZero.log_mul
408	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero u))
409	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero v))
410	
411	/-- **Principal divisors on a complete nonsingular curve have degree zero**
412	(Hartshorne Corollary II.6.10, Stacks 0BE3).
413	
414	For every nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×` on a smooth proper curve `C`
415	over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
416	`deg(div(f)) = 0 ∈ ℤ`.
417	
418	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_deg_zero` (Hartshorne II.6 Cor. 6.10 p. 138).
419	
420	The proof (Hartshorne 6.10): if `f ∈ k̄` is constant then `div(f) = 0` and the
421	claim is trivial. Otherwise the inclusion `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` exhibits `K(C)` as a
422	finite extension of `k̄(f) ≅ k̄(t)`, so the corresponding morphism
423	`φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` is finite, `div(f) = φ^*([0] - [∞])`, and pullback along a
424	finite morphism multiplies degree by `deg(φ)`. Two auxiliary sub-lemmas
425	(finite morphism induced by a non-constant rational function; multiplicativity
426	of degree under finite pullback, Hartshorne II.6.9) are deferred to follow-up
427	iters of `RR.1`. -/
428	theorem principal_degree_zero {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
429	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
430	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
431	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
432	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
433	    (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) :
434	    degree (principal f hf) = 0 := by
435	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10 (iter-178 partial). Case-split on whether every
436	  -- prime-divisor order of `f` vanishes. On a complete nonsingular curve,
437	  -- this case split exactly recovers Hartshorne's constant-vs-non-constant
438	  -- split: `(∀ Y, ord_Y f = 0)` ⟺ `f ∈ k̄ \ {0}` (a nowhere-vanishing
439	  -- rational function on a complete curve is constant; that direction is
440	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10's "if `f ∈ k̄` then `(f) = 0`").
441	  by_cases hconst : ∀ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
442	  · -- Constant branch: every order vanishes ⟹ `principal f hf = 0` ⟹
443	    -- `degree (principal f hf) = degree 0 = 0`.
444	    have hprincipal_zero : principal f hf = 0 := by
445	      apply Finsupp.ext
446	      intro Y
447	      change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
448	      exact hconst Y
449	    rw [hprincipal_zero]
450	    unfold degree
451	    exact Finsupp.sum_zero_index
452	  · -- Non-constant branch (Hartshorne II.6.10 `f ∉ k̄`): the inclusion
453	    -- `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` defines a finite morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the
454	    -- function-field-determines-curve correspondence (Hartshorne I.6.12),
455	    -- and `principal f hf = φ^*([0] - [∞])`. Pullback along a finite
456	    -- morphism of curves multiplies degrees by `deg(φ)` (Hartshorne
457	    -- II.6.9), so `deg(principal f hf) = deg(φ) · deg([0] - [∞]) =
458	    -- deg(φ) · 0 = 0`.
459	    -- This branch is gated on (i) the `φ : C → ℙ¹` construction (Lane 5
460	    -- `RationalCurveIso.lean:morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`, iter-178+),
461	    -- and (ii) the degree-multiplicativity-under-finite-pullback bridge
462	    -- (Hartshorne II.6.9, Mathlib gap).
463	    sorry
464	
465	/-! ## §6. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
466	
467	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
468	
469	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
470	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
471	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
472	
473	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
474	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
475	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
476	**divisor class group** of `X`.
477	
478	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
479	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
480	
481	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
482	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
483	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
484	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf
485	
486	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
487	
488	end AlgebraicGeometry

385	  apply hx01_ne

388	  rw [hsec_zero, map_zero]
389	  rfl
390	
391	/-- **The pole divisor of a morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹` of smooth proper curves
392	over `k̄`.** As a Weil divisor on `C`, it is `φ^*[∞]` — the pullback of the
393	divisor `[∞]` on `ℙ¹` along `φ`.
394	
395	**iter-187 substantive body (Path (a) Finsupp via principal divisor).**
396	The body is the principal Weil divisor on `C` of the pulled-back local
397	parameter `algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val`:
398	
399	  `Hom.poleDivisor φ := principal (algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) t_∞) _`
400	
401	Mathematically, `div(φ^* t_∞) = (φ^* t_∞)_0 − (φ^* t_∞)_∞` on `C`, where
402	`(φ^* t_∞)_0 = φ^*[∞]` (the zeros of `φ^* t_∞` are exactly the preimages
403	of `∞`, where `t_∞` vanishes on `ℙ¹`) and `(φ^* t_∞)_∞ = φ^*[0]` (the
404	poles are the preimages of `0`). For a non-constant `φ` the negative
405	part `(φ^* t_∞)_∞` of the principal divisor identifies the preimages of
406	`0` — disjoint from `φ⁻¹(∞)` in the standard convention — so the
407	positive part recovers `φ^*[∞]` on the nose. The full pole-divisor
408	identification `(φ^* t_∞)₀ = φ^*[∞]` is deferred to the iter-188+
409	follow-up; the iter-187 body lands the φ-dependent principal divisor as
410	the substantive Weil-divisor placeholder consumed by
411	`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` (whose degree-via-`finrank`
412	identification works modulo the deg-zero-of-`(φ^* t_∞)_∞` correction,
413	which is `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero`).
414	
415	The φ-dependence is carried by the `[Algebra K(ℙ¹) K(C)]` instance
416	binder, which at every call site is the canonical `φ`-induced

/-- **Positive part of a Weil divisor** (the lattice `D ⊔ 0`, equivalently the
pointwise `Y ↦ max(D Y, 0)` on the underlying `Finsupp`). For `D = div(f)` this
recovers the divisor-of-zeros `(f)_0 = positivePart (div f)`.

File-local iter-190 helper for the Pin 2 corrective; will migrate to

private noncomputable def WeilDivisor.positivePart {X : Scheme.{u}}
    (D : X.WeilDivisor) : X.WeilDivisor :=
  Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp) D

irreducible curves over `k̄` (algebraically closed), with the canonical

divisor `φ^*[∞] = positivePart (principal (φ^# t_∞))` equals

noncomputable def Hom.poleDivisor
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField]
    (_φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :
    C.left.WeilDivisor :=
  -- Pull a chosen local parameter `t_∞ ∈ K(ℙ¹)` back to `K(C)` along the
  -- canonical `φ`-induced algebra map, and take its principal divisor.
  -- The `t_∞ ≠ 0` ⟹ `algebraMap _ _ t_∞ ≠ 0` step is automatic from
  -- injectivity of the algebra map `K(ℙ¹) → K(C)` (a ring hom out of a
  -- field is injective).
  let t := localParameterAtInfty kbar
  have halg :
      algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
          C.left.functionField t.val ≠ 0 := by
    intro h
    apply t.property
    have hinj :
        Function.Injective
          (algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
            C.left.functionField) :=
      RingHom.injective _
    have h0 :
        algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
            C.left.functionField 0 = 0 :=
      RingHom.map_zero _
    exact hinj (h.trans h0.symm)
  Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
    (algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
      C.left.functionField t.val)
    halg

**iter-190 substantive body (Pin 2 corrective Option (a) — positive part of
principal).** The body is the *positive part* of the principal Weil divisor
on `C` of the pulled-back local parameter
`algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val`:

  `Hom.poleDivisor φ := positivePart (principal (algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) t_∞) _)`

Mathematically, `div(φ^* t_∞) = (φ^* t_∞)_0 − (φ^* t_∞)_∞` on `C`, where
`(φ^* t_∞)_0 = φ^*[∞]` (the zeros of `φ^* t_∞` are exactly the preimages
of `∞`, where `t_∞` vanishes on `ℙ¹`) and `(φ^* t_∞)_∞ = φ^*[0]` (the
poles are the preimages of `0`). Extracting the positive part of
`div(φ^* t_∞)` exactly recovers `φ^*[∞]` on the nose.

**Why the positive-part wrap is essential (Pin 2 structural-conflict
diagnosis, iter-189 → iter-190 corrective).** The naked principal divisor
`principal (algebraMap _ _ t_∞) halg` has degree zero on a complete
nonsingular curve (Hartshorne II.6.10 / Stacks 0BE3,
`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `WeilDivisor.lean`),
whereas the RHS `Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)` of
`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` is positive (= deg φ ≥ 1) at every
call site that uses the φ-induced algebra structure. The positive-part
wrap restores provability: `degree (positivePart (principal (φ^# t_∞)))
= degree (φ^*[∞]) = deg(φ) = [K(C):K(ℙ¹)]` (Hartshorne~II.6.9).

The φ-dependence is carried by the `[Algebra K(ℙ¹) K(C)]` instance
binder, which at every call site is the canonical `φ`-induced
function-field map (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2).
Changing the algebra instance changes the divisor; in particular, the
unfolding
`Hom.poleDivisor φ ⇝ positivePart (principal (algebraMap _ _ t) _)`
is visible to definitional unfolding, *not* opaque behind
`Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / empty `proof_wanted` placeholders.

**Helper budget**: 1 named typed sorry (`localParameterAtInfty`, the
local-parameter choice; substrate gap on the chart-`1` ratio coordinate
of `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`). The Pin 2 corrective additionally

pin `WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_localParameterAtInfty_eq_finrank`
above; these are scheduled to migrate to the public
`Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` /
`Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` of
`WeilDivisor.lean` once the parallel iter-190 WeilDivisor prover lands
the public versions.

Blueprint reference: `def:Hom.poleDivisor` (Hartshorne IV §2 opening +
II §6.9; chapter `chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso` §2 "Convention on
the pole divisor"); iter-190 plan-phase Pin 2 corrective NOTE on
`lem:degree_via_pole_divisor`. -/
noncomputable def Hom.poleDivisor
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField]
    (_φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :
    C.left.WeilDivisor :=
  -- Pull a chosen local parameter `t_∞ ∈ K(ℙ¹)` back to `K(C)` along the
  -- canonical `φ`-induced algebra map, take its principal divisor, then
  -- extract the positive part (i.e. the zeros, which on a complete curve
  -- coincide with `φ^*[∞]`). The `t_∞ ≠ 0` ⟹ `algebraMap _ _ t_∞ ≠ 0`
  -- step is automatic from injectivity of the algebra map
  -- `K(ℙ¹) → K(C)` (a ring hom out of a field is injective).
  let t := localParameterAtInfty kbar
  have halg :
      algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
          C.left.functionField t.val ≠ 0 := by
    intro h
    apply t.property
    have hinj :
        Function.Injective
          (algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
            C.left.functionField) :=
      RingHom.injective _
    have h0 :
        algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
            C.left.functionField 0 = 0 :=
      RingHom.map_zero _
    exact hinj (h.trans h0.symm)
  WeilDivisor.positivePart
    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
      (algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
        C.left.functionField t.val)
      halg)

1	# Analogy: Pin 3 — `iso_of_degree_one` (Hartshorne I.6.12 / Stacks 0AVX)
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ratcurveiso-pins (Pin 3)
8	
9	## Iteration
10	181
11	
12	## Question
13	
14	Does Mathlib have either of (a) a "degree-1 morphism is iso" lemma for
15	smooth proper curves, (b) `IsBirational` for scheme morphisms with the
16	"birational ⟹ iso for smooth proper schemes" specialisation, (c)
17	Zariski's main theorem at a level the project can consume directly? If
18	yes, the project should align with the Mathlib idiom for
19	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one`
20	(`AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean:356-369`).
21	
22	## Project artifact(s)
23	
24	- `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean:356-369` —
25	  `theorem iso_of_degree_one` with body `sorry`. The statement asserts
26	  `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` from `φ : C ⟶ C'` non-constant + the existence of
27	  a ring iso `C'.left.functionField ≃+* C.left.functionField`.
28	
29	## Decisions identified
30	
31	Two coupled decisions:
32	
33	1. Does Mathlib have a `IsBirational` predicate or the specialised
34	   theorem "birational ⟹ iso for smooth proper integral schemes (or
35	   curves)"?
36	2. Does Mathlib have any wrapper of Zariski's main theorem / Hartshorne
37	   III.11.4 / Stacks 0AVX at the curve level?
38	
39	### Decision 1: birational ⟹ iso (for smooth proper curves)
40	
41	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE for the named theorem. Search confirms no
42	  `IsBirational`, no `Hartshorne.III_11_4`, no `ZariskiMainTheorem`,
43	  no "degree-1 ⟹ iso" specialisation. The closest Mathlib has:
44	
45	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Normalization` —
46	    `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization f` and the instance
47	    `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`
48	    (`QuasiCompact f` + `QuasiSeparated f` + `IsIntegralHom f` ⟹
49	    `IsIso (Scheme.Hom.toNormalization f)`). The normalisation =
50	    the target when the target is already normal. Smooth ⟹ regular ⟹
51	    normal in dim 1, so this lemma is *almost* what is needed — but it
52	    proves the *normalisation map* is an iso, not the original morphism.
53	    Bridging it would need to identify `Scheme.Hom.normalization f` with
54	    `C` itself (under the smooth-proper-curve hypothesis) and route the
55	    final iso through the normalisation universal property.
56	
57	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RationalMap`:
58	    `Scheme.RationalMap.ofFunctionField`,
59	    `Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField`. These give the rational-map
60	    side of "function-field iso ⟹ birational map", but the *extension
61	    of a rational map to a morphism* (the "smooth-target / proper-source
62	    extension" theorem) is also absent from Mathlib for the
63	    smooth-proper-curve setting.
64	
65	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion.isIso_iff_stalk_iso` /
66	    `isIso_iff_isIso_stalkMap`: `f` is iso iff `f.base` is iso *and*
67	    each stalk map is iso. This is the cleanest Mathlib criterion to
68	    reduce the goal to (stalks + topological iso).
69	
70	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.IsIso.isomorphisms_eq_stalkwise`
71	    / `isIso_iff_isOpenImmersion_and_surjective` — alternative
72	    iso criteria via stalks + open immersion / surjective.
73	
74	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.ClosedImmersion.isIso_of_isClosedImmersion_of_surjective`:
75	    closed immersion + surjective + reduced target ⟹ iso. This is a
76	    convenient hammer when the morphism is closed-image.
77	
78	- **Project's current path**: the lemma takes the function-field iso as
79	  a hypothesis (`Nonempty (C'.left.functionField ≃+* C.left.functionField)`)
80	  and outputs `Nonempty (C ≅ C')`. The lemma body is `sorry` and the
81	  docstring (L342-352) lays out two routes: (a) the coherent-sheaf
82	  argument (`φ_* O_C` is a coherent `O_{C'}`-module of generic rank 1,
83	  inclusion `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is an iso of rank-1 torsion-free sheaves
84	  on a Dedekind base), (b) the equivalence-of-categories argument
85	  (smooth proj curves ≃ function fields).
86	
87	- **Gap**: divergent-and-isolated. Mathlib does not package
88	  "birational ⟹ iso for smooth proper curves" as a named lemma. The
89	  closest is `instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`, but it proves
90	  iso of the normalisation map, not of the original morphism. The
91	  bridge from there to Pin 3 is non-trivial:
92	  - Show `φ : C ⟶ C'` is finite (proper + locally-finite-type +
93	    quasi-finite via the degree-1 hypothesis collapsed at the generic
94	    point; alternatively, finite-on-each-affine-chart via integral
95	    closure). Mathlib has `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`, so
96	    once affine-hom is established, finiteness follows.
97	  - Show `Scheme.Hom.normalization φ = C` (i.e. the normalisation of
98	    `φ` is `C` itself, since `C` is normal/smooth). Then
99	    `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization φ : C ⟶ normalization φ` is an iso,
100	    and `fromNormalization : normalization φ ⟶ C'` is the integral
101	    closure inclusion `O_{C'} ↪ O_C` viewed as a morphism of schemes.
102	  - Under the degree-1 hypothesis (function-field iso), the integral
103	    closure `O_{C'} ↪ O_C` becomes the identity, so `fromNormalization`
104	    is an iso (and `φ = toNormalization ≫ fromNormalization` is then
105	    a composition of isos).
106	
107	- **Cost of divergence**: medium. The Mathlib normalisation lemma
108	  exists but the bridging argument (identify the normalisation of `φ`
109	  with `C` via smoothness; show the degree-1 hypothesis collapses the
110	  integral closure to identity) is ~80-150 LOC of new project
111	  infrastructure.
112	
113	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** (with `instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`
114	  + `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom` as a candidate body
115	  strategy; a coherent-rank-1 sheaf strategy is the alternative,
116	  arguably cleaner but requires more sheaf-cohomology API the project
117	  has not exposed). Final route choice is iter-178+ planner's call.
118	
119	### Decision 2: signature shape — function-field iso hypothesis
120	
121	- **Mathlib idiom**: when stating "morphism induces iso on function
122	  fields", Mathlib's convention is to state things in terms of
123	  `Scheme.functionField` as a `CommRingCat` (it's the *literal* field
124	  at the generic point, not a `Field` type-synonym). The induced
125	  function-field map for a `φ : X ⟶ Y` of integral schemes would be the
126	  composite
127	  `Y.functionField → Y.presheaf.stalk (φ.base ξ_X) →[stalkMap]→
128	   X.presheaf.stalk ξ_X → X.functionField`
129	  using `germToFunctionField` + `IsFractionRing.lift`. There is no
130	  named `Scheme.Hom.functionFieldMap` in Mathlib — this is a small
131	  derived definition the project would write inline.
132	
133	- **Project's current path**: the lemma takes an *abstract* ring iso
134	  `Nonempty (C'.left.functionField ≃+* C.left.functionField)` as a
135	  hypothesis, NOT specifically the iso induced by `φ`. This is a
136	  deliberate weakening (per the docstring L334-341): "the existence
137	  wrapper matches the file-skeleton's substantive-but-non-tautological-
138	  type discipline without committing prematurely to a specific Mathlib
139	  formulation of the canonical function-field map."
140	
141	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost (mild). The hypothesis is *strictly
142	  weaker* than needed for the iter-178+ body to extract a useful
143	  birational map: any ring iso `K(C') ≃+* K(C)` will do, but the
144	  birational-extension argument needs the iso induced by `φ` itself.
145	  Concretely, the existence hypothesis tells you nothing about how
146	  the abstract field iso relates to `φ`'s action on the generic point.
147	
148	- **Cost of divergence**: medium. The iter-178+ body will need to
149	  *strengthen* the hypothesis (or replace it with a non-constancy +
150	  degree-from-field-extension formulation) before the
151	  normalisation-based argument can proceed.
152	
153	- **Verdict**: **DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY**, but flag for re-statement
154	  during iter-178+ body work. The current existence hypothesis is a
155	  file-skeleton placeholder; the planner should anticipate refining
156	  it to either (a) "`φ` induces a function-field iso" (using the
157	  canonical stalk-map-at-generic-point construction inline), or
158	  (b) "`φ` has function-field-extension degree 1"
159	  (`Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`).
160	
161	## Recommendation
162	
163	Both decisions land **PROCEED**: Mathlib has no
164	"birational ⟹ iso for smooth proper curves" theorem, no `IsBirational`,
165	no Zariski's main theorem wrapper at the curve level. The project's
166	in-tree route is the correct call.
167	
168	For the iter-178+ body, the planner should:
169	
170	1. Plan to **refine the hypothesis** of `iso_of_degree_one` from
171	   "abstract function-field iso" to "function-field map induced by
172	   `φ` is iso" — this is needed to make the birational argument go
173	   through. Either by adding a wrapper `Scheme.Hom.functionFieldMap`
174	   (8-15 LOC: composite of stalkMap-at-generic-point and
175	   IsFractionRing.lift) or by replacing the existence hypothesis
176	   with `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`.
177	2. Plan the body via the **normalisation route**:
178	   - Reduce to `φ` finite (proper + quasi-finite ⟹ finite via
179	     `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`; quasi-finiteness comes
180	     from the degree-1 hypothesis collapsing each fibre).
181	   - Use `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` to get
182	     `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization φ : C ⟶ normalization φ` is an iso.
183	   - Identify `normalization φ = C'` via smoothness of `C'` (smooth
184	     proper curve over algebraically closed field ⟹ regular ⟹
185	     normal). The integral closure `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is then an
186	     equality under the degree-1 hypothesis.
187	   - Compose to extract `C ≅ C'`.
188	
189	Budget: ~80-150 LOC for the body, of which ~30-50 LOC is the
190	"normalisation of `φ` = C' under smooth/normal" bridge.
191	
192	Alternative route (sheaf-cohomology): show `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is iso
193	of coherent rank-1 torsion-free sheaves on a smooth proper Dedekind
194	base. Cleaner conceptually but requires more sheaf API the project has
195	not exposed.
196	
197	## Possible Mathlib gap-fill (informational, not blocking)
198	
199	For a future upstream PR, the following would close the gap in Mathlib:
200	
201	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.functionFieldMap : (f : X ⟶ Y)
202	  [IsIntegral X] [IsIntegral Y] [IsDominant f] :
203	  Y.functionField →+* X.functionField`.
204	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsBirational : (f : X ⟶ Y) → Prop` and the
205	  theorem "smooth + proper + integral + birational ⟹ iso" (Hartshorne
206	  III.11.4 / Stacks 0AVX).
207	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.isIso_of_finrank_functionField_one`:
208	  a degree-1 finite morphism of integral schemes is iso.
209	
210	The project must not block on any of these — the in-tree route is the
211	correct call for iter-181/182.
